inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Mūlasthānēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Built in and incomplete. The inscription commences with the praśasti
of the king and stops with the mention of name, Chaḍaiyapaṉmar.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] pūviṉkiḻatti mevi vīṟṟiruppa metiṉi mātu nītiyiṟ puṇara vayap
por maṭantai jaya Built in at the end.-

lai maṭantai vākkiṉil viḷaṅkat ticaiyirunāṉku micainilā veṟippa
maṟaineṟi vaḷar maṉuneṟi tikaḻa Built in at the end.-

ṟun taḻaippa kāṉaveṅkaiyai villuṭaṉṟurantu mīṉaḷaṅ kaṉakācaṉattu
vīṟṟiruppa eṇkiri cūḻnta eḻuka Built in at the end.-

l veṇkuṭai niḻaṟṟac ceṅkol naṭappa koṭuṅkali naṭuṅki neṭumpilat
toḷippa villavar [cem]piyar virāṭa Built in at the end.-

tiṟaiyuṭaṉ muṟaimuṟai paṇiya iru nemiyaḷavu moru nemiyoṅka
iṉṉamutākiya iyalicai nāṭaka maṉṉi Built in at the end.-

viḷaṅkiya katiroḷi virasimhāsanattu vīṟṟiruntaruḷiya śrī
koccaṭaiyapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉac cakkaravattika Built in at the end.-

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Mūlasthānēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Seems to record some gift of provisions to
the Tānattār of the Mūlasthāṉamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār temple, for smearing on
to the body of the diety, Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa Nāchchiyār, under orders of
Narasiṅgadēvaṉ.

l.ta: naraciṅka teva[ṉ] Olai uṭaiyār śrī mūlattāṉamuṭaiya nāyaṉār

ṉār yeḻuntaruḷuvitta tirukkāmakkoṭṭa nācciyārkku tirumel
pūccukkum tiruppā-

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Mūlasthānēśvara temple.

p.b: Māṟavarmaṉ Parākramapāṇḍya. Year 11 and 90th day: A.D. 1155.

inscription.p:
Tax free gift of the village of Cheṅguḷam Māpiraiyaviṭaṅkanallūr to
the temple of Mūlasthāṉamuḍaiya Mahādēva at the request of the saint
Chēramāṉ Tōḻaṉ by the king. The order of the king was issued when he
was seated on his throne Isaiyaḷavukaṇḍāṉ in the hall Māṇābaraṇaṉ in his
palace at Madurai. Signatures of officers of the Revenue (Puravuvari)
department are affixed at the end of the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.

p.b: No king. (Māṟavarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya I). Year 13+1+1: A.D. 1230.

inscription.p:
This inscription is related to No. 133 below. Some portions of the
inscription could not be traced. The inscription records the royal order
issued by the king M. Sundarapāṇḍya I, assigning 3(1/2) vēlis as dēvadāṉa
iraiyili to the temple of Śrīvallava Viṇṇagar Āḻvār on the western bank
(of the Vaigai river ?) at Chōḻakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pāgaṉūr-
kūṟṟam for offerings. The taxes exempted are antarāyam, viṉiyōkam and
mākkalam. The uḷvari was signed by three officers.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
In Grantha characters and seems to be related to No. 134 below.
Records a gift of land to the temple of Madhūbid (i.e., Vishṇu) in the
village of Tēṉūr alias Jananātha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by Kōdaṇḍa Chōḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a third pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription is related to No. 131 above. Though the name of the
king is not mentioned in the inscription he may be identified with
Sundara Pāṇḍya I with reference to the inscription since the same officer
(Maḻavarāyaṉ) appear in both the inscriptions.

Records an order of the king issued to śrīkāryam and śrīvaishaṇavas
in the temple of Śrivallava Viṇṇagar Ālvār on the western bank (of the
Vaigai river ?), in the Chōḻakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pāgaṉūr-
kūṟṟam. A piece of land measuring 3(1/2) vēlis was granted as dēvadāṉa
iṟaiyili for tiruppaḍimāṟṟu offerings in the temple on a representation
made by Maḻavarāyaṉ. The taxes exempted are antarāyam, viṉiyōkam and
mākkalam. The uḷvari was signed by three officers.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a fourth pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Gift of lands situated at Perundēṉūr by Jananātha
Pallavaraiyaṉ alias Kōdaṇḍaṉ Chōlai to the temple Srī Vīrakēraḷa
Viṇṇagardēvar in Jananātha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Pāgaṉūr-kūṟṟam for
offerings in the temple. (see No. 132 above).

edition.l: 17 āvatu [ viruda] rāja vaḷanā-

l.ta: ttu naṭuvil śrī koyil śrī vī-

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a fifth pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Mithuna, śu., 13, Wednesday, Anila = A.D. 1192, May 27, Wednesday.).

Gift of half vēli of land by the assembly of Chōḷāntaka-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Pāgaṉūr-kūṟṟam to the mendicants
(paricārakar) of the Yōgasthāna of Kaṟṟavar-dāsar, situated in the village.
The grant was resolved by the mahājanas, bhaṭṭas and managers of the
village, in a meeting held at the Vīrapāṇḍiyaṉ hall and the tax, sabhā-
viṉiyōgam on the land was also exempted. The long list of signatories of
village functionaries included two accountants and two sculptors
(silpāchāris), the latter members engraved the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On the sixth pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records that the pillar was erected by Kaṇḍaṉ Udaiyaṉ of
Vallanagara, a village in Malai-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 136A.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On the seventh pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records that the pillar was erected by Kaṇḍaṉ Udaiyaṉ of
Vallanagara, a village in Malai-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a slab lying in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Much damaged and mutilated. Seems to refer to the temple of
Mūlasthāṉēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a stone set up in the courtyard of the Mūlasthānēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 251.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1910.)
TEṈKARAI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURA DISTRICT.
On a pillar in the gōpura of the Mūlasthāṉēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and mutilated. The beginning portion of the inscription is
lost. It contains portions of historical introduction of the king.

edition.l:
pallavar mutali[ya*] pārt-
(few lines missing)

koṭuttaruḷip pāṭaruñ-
(few lines missing)

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1910.)
SHIYALI, SHIYALI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On a slab excavated at Kāvērippaṭṭanam and now preserved in the Taluk
office at Shiyali.

p.b: Dated in the cyclic year Yuva.: A.D. 1815 (?).

inscription.p:
Grant of land for performing bathing ceremony (abhshēka-kaṭṭaḷai) of
the deity Kuḍaṅgkoṇḍa Nāyiṉār for the merit of Ādimutti Ayyaṉ.

edition.l: piṣeka kaṭṭa[ ḷai
yu] kku oṭṭuk-

inscription.head:
No. 140A.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1910.)
SHIYALI, SHIYALI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On a slab excavated at Kāvērippaṭṭanam and now preserved in the Taluk
office at Shiyali.
inscription.p:
Seems to record a grant of a hall, sacred tank, and a temple garden
in the street Rāyanārāyaṇa to the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p: (Chitrabhānu, Vaikāsi, 15, śu., 5, Friday, Pūsam = A.D. 1342, May 10, Friday.)

Assignment of 20 mā of land as kuḍinīṅgāt-tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam to the
temple of Tiruvulagaḷanda Perumāḷ in the village. The assembly of Veḷḷalūr
alias Sōmīsaradēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pērūr-nāḍu agreed to pay the
tax dues fixed on permanent basis (vāḍākkaḍamai) for the whole year
amounting to forty paṇams and four hundred kalams of paddy at the gate
of tirumaḍaippaḷḷi. Mentions the grain measure tiruvāṉaik-kāl.

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the right side of east entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of the
central shrine in the Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a grant for worship and
repairs in the temple of Aviṉāsi Āḷuḍaiyār at Tiruppukkoḷiyūr by the three
classes of people residing in Aṉṉadāṉasivapuri alias Veḷḷalūr in Pērūr-nāḍu.

edition.l: [svastiśrī tiripu]

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the right side of east entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of the
central shrine in the Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a gift for the merit of
Kōvaṉ Arachaṉ, the son-in-law of Arachanukkap Pallavaraiyan.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the right side of south entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of
the central shrine in the Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of one achchu for two evening lamps (chandyā dīpam) to be
lighted perpetually in the temple of Teṉṉūrpadi by Cheṭṭi, the wife of
Mūkkaṉ, a merchant of Maṉaiyāmpaḍi in Vāyaṟaikkāl-nāḍu. The two śiva-
brāhmaṇas who had the kāṇi right in the temple undertook to maintain
the lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tēṉīśvara
temple.
inscription.p: Damaged. Gift of a pillar by an individual whose name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Records the gift of a pillar in the maṇḍapa by
Toḷaikōvaṉ alias Arachayan.

edition.l: yāṇṭu 6-

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The king has been attributed as born in the Chandrakula. Gift of the
stone pillar in the maṇḍapa of Teṉṉūr Nakkaṉār temple at Veḷilūr
(Veḷḷalūr), by Mūrkka Pēraṇḍan for the merit of his father, Eṟamūr
Nakkaṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of the stone pillar in the maṇḍapa, of the Teṉṉūr Nakkanār
temple at Veḷilūr (Veḷḷalūr) by Kaṇṇaṅkāvan.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1910.)
VEḶḶALŪR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the fifth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine
in the Tēṉīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. Gift of gold for lighting 20 lamps by
Uḍaiyanāyagaṉ Tuṇaiyirundāṉ of Tañjāvūr-kūṟṟam, in Pāṇḍikulāsani-
vaḷanāḍu which was a subdivision of Chōḻa-maṇḍalam to the temple of
Tēṉūr Āṇḍār at Veḷḷalūr in Pērūr-nāḍu. The two śiva-brāhmaṇas of the
temple agreed to maintain lamps in the temple during the tiruppaḷḷi-
eḻuchchi ceremony for thirty days.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the central shrine in the Nāgēśvara temple.

Records that Kāḷividiyāṉ Sundarapperumāḷ, a kaikkōla resident of
Adirādarāchaṉ tirumaḍaiviḷāgam, erected two door-posts in the temple of
Tirunāgīchchuram Uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of land measuring one kāṇi in extent for providing sacred food
offerings (poriyaval tiruppaṇṇikāram) to the presiding deity in the
Tirunāgīśvaram Uḍaiyār temple at Muṭṭam alias Amarabuyaṅgaranallūr in
Pērūr-nāḍu by Viḻumipuṭṭiyāṉ Daṉapālaṉ, a maṉṟāḍi of the local village.
An irrigation tank, Śrī Adisayachōḻap-pērēri, and two canals, Vīranārāyaṇa-
vāykkāḷ and Uttamasīli-vāykkal are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of half mā of land for providing puffed rice (poṟi),
and other sacred food offerings during some ceremony (tiruttāḷa kūṭṭam), to
the deity in the Tirunāgīśvaram-Uḍaiyār temple at Muṭṭam alias
Amarabuyaṅgaranallūr in Pērūr-nāḍu by Viḻumipuṭṭiyāṉ Daṉapālaṉ. An
irrigation canal, Vīranārāyaṇa-vāykkāl is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and mutilated at the end. Mentions the temple
Tirunāgīchchuram and one maṉṟāḍi, Arachaṉ Viḻumiyār alias Sivapattaṉ.

l.ta:
ṭattu maṉṟāṭi kamukakkuṭiyuṭaiyārkaḷil aracaṉ viḻumiyārāṉa

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. The inscription is a copy of an old
inscription. Seems to register gifts of land made by Rājarājadēva, for some
services in the temple at Muṭṭam. Mentions the villages, Kōḷūr alias
Arumoḻidēvach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, Irācarāca-nallūr, Iravivaṉmach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam and Amarabhuyaṅgara-nallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Nāgēśvari shrine in the
Nāgēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Built in at the beginning and stones out of order. Gift
of four achchu for sacred food offerings to the deity Tirukkoṭidēvar in the
temple of Tirunāgīchchuram Uḍaiyār at Muṭṭam alias Iravivaṉmach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, by a servant, Kāḷi Bemmaṉ from
[A]marabuyaṅgaranallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the Veṅkaṭēśvara Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of one achchu for a twilight lamp (chandhyā dīpam) to be
lighted perpetually in the temple of Kulasēkara Viṇṇagar Emberumāṉ by a
lady Uyyān Chaḍaichchi.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1910.)
BŌḶUMĀMPAṬṬI (Ikkarai Bōḷuvāmpaṭṭi), COIMBATORE TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the Veṅkaṭēśvara Perumāḷ temple.

p.b: No king or date. (14th cent. A.D.)

inscription.p:
Gift of one achchu for maintaining one twilight lamp (sandhyā
dīpam) to be lighted perpetually in the temple of Kulasēkara Viṇṇagar
Emberumāṉ by Maṭṭi Āṇḍāṉ alias Dēsivāraṇaṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of one achchu for a twilight lamp (chandhyā dīpam) by Mōḍan
Cheṭṭi alias Īsānadēvaṉ, a merchant from Kāṅgayam to the temple of
Kulōttuṅgachōḻīśvaram Uḍaiyār at Muttūr. The lamp shall be maintained by
Kācuvan Bhaṭṭan Uḍaiyāṉ alias Kuladīpa Bhaṭṭan, a śiva-brāhmaṇa holding
the service right (kāṇi) in the temple and his descendants. (see Nos. 159,
165, 166).

l.ta: maṇaṟk kācuvan paṭṭan uṭaiyāṉāna kulatīpa pa-

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Gift of one achchu for a lamp by a merchant Chaḍaiyan
Cheṭṭi. The lamp shall be maintained by Kācuvan Bhaṭṭan Uḍaiyāṉ alias
Kuladīpa Bhaṭṭan, a śiva-brāhmaṇa of the temple and his descendants.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a gift to the temple of
Kulōttuṅgachōḻīśvaram Uḍaiyār at Muttūr in Kāṅgaiya-nāḍu for food
offerings.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Registers the king's order issued to
Pāppārchaṉṟāṟ of Muttūr that he shall provide sufficient amount of rice
for food offerings to the Kulōttuṅgachōḻīśvaram Uḍaiyār temple in lieu of
taxes (iṟai) payable by him.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Mentions Muttūr in Kāṅgaiya-nāḍu. On the
left margin the syllables Rājākēsari are engraved.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Records that Cheviḍai Periyāṉ, Naḍuviṟ Periyāṉ and
Chiṟup Periyāṉ, the three potters (vēṭkōvaṉ) who held the right of
supplying earthern ware pots to the temple of Muttūr in Kāṅgaiya-nāḍu
received one and half achchu from the authorities of the temple of
Kulōttuṅgachōḻīśvaram Uḍaiyār and probably agreed to supply earthern
ware vessels to the temple.

l.ta: ṉu naṭuviṟ periyāṉu ciṟup periyāṉum muttū-

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of one twilight lamp (chandhyā dīpam) to be lighted in the
temple at Muttūr by Uḍaiyapirāṭṭi, the wife of Cheyyāṉ Vaiyyāṉ, a
resident of Koṭṭaṉūr in Teṉkarai-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of one achchu for a twilight lamp (chandhyā dīpam) to be
lighted in the shrine of Periyanāchchiyār by Chiṅgaṉ alias Aṅgarāyaṉ, the
son of Uttamachōḻap pallavaraiyan of Muttūr. Kācuvan Bhaṭṭan Uḍaiyan
alias Kuladīpa Bhaṭṭan, a śiva-brāhmaṇa of the Periya Nāchchiyār temple
and his descendants shall maintain the lamp. (see Nos. 158, 166).

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1910.)
MUTTŪR, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḻēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to register a gift of six achchus by the nagarattār of
Muttūr to the temple of Kulōttuṅgachōḻichcharam Uḍaiyār for some
services in the temple. Kuladīpa Bhaṭṭan, a śiva-brāhmaṇa of the temple
holding the right to temple service (kāṇi) gave an undertaking to the
nagarattār to maintain the grant. (see Nos. 158, 159, 165)

l.ta: ttār pakkal tirupputiyituk • • ṇṭa accu pa-
kal veṭṭik kuṭutten kulatīpa paṭṭanen muttū • • •

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1910.)
ERŌḌE, ERODE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the door-posts of the entrance into the central shrine in the
Raghunāthasvāmi temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription seems to be a copy re-engraved in the 13th century.
Records that the Nāṭṭavar of Ēḻugarait Tiruvāyppāḍi agreed to pay to the
deity of Krishṇa (Veṇṇaikkūtta Nāyaṉār) in the temple of Paḷḷikoṇḍāḻvār
at Īrōḍu (Erode) certain local taxes levied on the people of the locality to
defray the expenses for the supply of sacred food offerings in the temple.
The levy was imposed at the rate of half paṇam on each household (kuḍi)
and one eighth (1/8) paṇam on each bride and bridegroom. One kuṉṟi and
mañcāṭi (3/4 kācu) was levied as cuṭukāṭṭuppāṭṭam at the place of
cremation.

Published in S.I.I., vol. XXXII, pt. I, No. 20; ERMK., Vol. I, No.
1008/2003, p. 27-28.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1910.)
ERŌḌE, ERODE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Ardrakapālēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1426, Rudrodgari, Paṅkuṉi 25, Wednesday, Rōhiṇi = A.D. 1504, March
21, Thursday.).

Gift of 20 mā of land for undertaking repairs and conducting festival
in the temple of Toṇḍīśvaramuḍaiya Mahādēva at Īrōḍu (Erōḍe) in
Kāñchittuṇḍam, a subdivision of Mēlkarai Pūndurai-nāḍu by the Chief.
Mentions one mā of land consited of 256 kuḻis measured by the measuring
rod with a length of 24 aḍi (length of human foot).
Published in ERMK., Vol. I, No. 1009/2003, p. 29.

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1910.)
ERŌḌE, ERODE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Ardrakapālēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The given details of date correspond to A.D. 1510. However, the
Kaliyuga quoted in the inscription is wrong. Instead of Śaka, the Sālivāhaṇa
era has been named. However the cyclic year Prajōtpati, Tai, pūrva
paksha, 7th day Monday, uttirattādi, corresponds to A.D. 1510.

Damaged. Gift of 15 dēvarāyaṉ poṉ, to Toṇḍīśvaramuḍaiya-Tambirānār
at Īrōḍu (Erōḍe) in Kāñchittuṇḍam, a subdivision of Mēlkarai-nāḍu which
was a district of Koṅgu-maṇḍalam, for providing food offerings during the
makara-saṅkrānti period by Ādiyappa Gavuṇḍaṉ Mallai •••Gavuṇḍan, one of
the members of Okkal community from Ummattūr-sīrmai. The money
was received by two officials of the temple and out of the interest
accrued from the deposit they agreed to provide some food items to the
deity without fail. The interest for one poṉ was calculated at the rate of
10 paṇam per month.
Published in ERMK., Vol. I, No. 1010/2003, p. 30-31.

sōmavāramum uttiraṭṭātiyum pūrvvattu saptamiyum peṟṟa nāḷ koṅku

ātiyaṟaiyil okkal makkaḷil ātiyappa kavuṇṭaṉ mallai •••kavuṇṭaṉeṉ

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1910.)
ERŌḌE, ERODE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Ardrakapālēśvara temple, right of the Dvārapālakā image.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 705.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1910.)
SATYAMAṄGALAM, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On the basement of the Mīṉākshi-Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Stones out of order, damaged and incomplete. Seems to mention some
activities of potters (vēṟkōvar) from Tiṅgaḷūr in Kuṟuppan-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1910.)
SATYAMAṄGALAM, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On the basement of the Mīṉākshi-Sundarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Stones out of order. Seems to refer to a gift of 3 achchus (?) for
providing sacred food during the noon service (uchchi-chandhi) in the
temple of Kailāyamuḍaiyār by a member of veḷḷāḷan piḷḷar community
from Kuḷappalūr.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1910.)
KOḌUVĒLI (Koḍivēri), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On a pillar lying near the Bhavāṉi anicut.
inscription.p:
Portions damaged. Gift of some money (achchu) for a twilight lamp
in the temple of Tirumunnai-Āḷuḍaiyār, by Daruman of chiruttalai vēṭṭuvar
community from Teṉkarai Koḍuvēli in Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu. On accepting the
money as deposit, Āḷavatta Bhaṭṭan, a śiva-brāhmaṇa of the temple agreed
to maintain the lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1910.)
KOḌUVĒLI (Koḍivēri), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On a slab lying near the Bhavāṉi anicut.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Registers that the kāṇi rights in the temples of
Tirumunnaiyāḷuḍaiyār and Srī Bhūtaṉ Munnamuraippāṉ were sold for
money by two śiva-brāhmaṇas, Tiruvēgampamuḍaiyān Piḷḷaiyāḻvi and
Piḷḷaiyāḻvi Tiruvēgampamuḍaiyāṉ of Kāsu-gōtram from Teṉkarai Koḍuvēli
in Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1910.)
EṞAGANAHAḶḶI, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On a slab lying near a well.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 545.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1910.)
EṞAGANAHAḶḶI, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On another slab set up near a well.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 596.

p.b: No king. cyclic year Bhava in Śaka [13]16, = A.D. 1394.

inscription.p:
Much damaged. The figures of cow and calf are carved on the
upper portion of the slab.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1910.)
EṞAGANAHAḶḶI, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On a slab set up near the pond west of the village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 546.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1910.)
EṞAGANAHAḶḶI, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE
DISTRICT.
On three sections of a hero stone (vīrakkal) near the pipul tree,
west of the village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 420.

p.b: No king. cyclic year Parithāvi = A.D. 1552 (?).

inscription.p:
This damaged records seems to register a settlement between the
gauḍas of Ummattūr and the kuruba gauḍas of Hidināḍa-śīme.

l.ka: ṁ va kuṟuba ga·uḍagaḷige koṭṭa śilāśā[sa]

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1910.)
ŚIVĀYAPĀḶAYIAM, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On a stone set up on the way to the Bhavāṉi river near the village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 703.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyurgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Much damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a gift for
performing bathing ceremony of the goddess Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai Nāchchiyār in
the temple of Amaraviṭaṅka Perumāḷ at Pāriyūr in Kāñchikūval-nāḍu.

l.ta: kāñcikkūva ṉāṭṭu pāriyū[ri]l uṭaiyār amaraviṭaṅkap pe-

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the base of east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central
shrine in the Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and badly damaged. Seems to record a gift of land for
sacred food offerings to the deity.

ṉilam k[ā]va[lū]r vayyilpaṭṭi [i]raṇaraṇṭukku ellaiyāvatu kiḻakkup

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the east, north and west bases of the maṇḍapa in front of the
central shrine in the Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 12 paṇams by Piḷḷaiyappaṉ of Pāriyūr, a veḷḷāḷa from
Kāñchikkūval-nāḍu to the temple of Amaraviṭaṅkap Pe[rumāḷ] for probably
conducting the early morning awakening (paḷḷieḻuchchi) ceremony of the
deity. Three śiva-brāhmaṇas of the temple agreed to maintain the grant.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the east and north bases of the maṇḍapa in front of the central
shrine in the Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.

p.b: Kulōttuṅga III. Year 3 and 10th day: A.D. 1182.

inscription.p:
Records an order of the king remitting the tax voṭṭachchu on more
than 20 Nāḍus, and 7 Śaiva temples in the Vaḍa Koṅgu country. The
order stipulates some conditions for its adherence and one of the conditions
was that Sāmantas who conquer the said territories in future shall not
demand tax.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Slightly damaged. Registers that the kaṇmāḷars of the Kāñchikūval-
nāḍu were granted with privilege of using double conch and drum (bērigai)
on good and bad occasions and also the use of footware and plastering
(chāndu) of their houses. They were also permitted to inscribe this order
in places of their interest.

l.ta: kkūval nāṭṭil kaṇmāḷa[r]ku patinañcāvatu taṅkaḷukku naṉmai tiṉ-
maikku iraṭṭaic caṅkum ūti perikai uḷḷiṭṭana[vai]k koṭṭivittu-

k koḷ[ḷa]vu[m] tāṅkaḷ puṟappaṭaveṇṭum iṭaṅkaḷukku [pāta]rakṣai
kottu k[o]-

ḷḷavum [taṅkaḷ] vīṭukaḷ cāntiṭṭuk [k]oḷḷavum connom ippaṭikku
nammolai [piṭi]-

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
A royal order issued to the sthānathār of the temple of Amaraviṭaṅka
Perumāḷ at Pāriyūr. Araiyarkuḷam, a dilapidated irrigation tank was
granted by the king as dēvadāṉa iṟaiyili. The proceeds from the lands shall
be used for providing sacred food and for performing other services in the
temple during the sandhi instituted in the name of the king and also
during the Mārgaḻi festival in the temple.

l.ta: ttu payirum ēṟṟi aṉupavikkavum ippaṭikku kallilum

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription is related to the previous one (No. 187) and records an
order of the king transferring the dilapidated tank, as dēvadāṉa iṟaiyili to
the temple of Amaraviṭaṅgap-perumāḷ at Pāriyūr. It was stipulated that the
tank shall be repaired and the water from the tank to be utilized for the
cultivation of crops and one-third of the produce shall be used to meet the
requirements of the festival established in the name of the king.

l.ta: ti[kaḷ*] konerimai koṇṭāṉ pāriyūr ūrumūrāḷikaḷukku taṅka-
ḷ nāyaṉār amaraviṭaṅkap perumāḷ tevatāṉa uṭaikuḷamāy
kiṭaṉtapaṭiyāl namperāṟ kaṭṭiṉa cantikkum mārkaḻit tirunā[ḷu]-
ṅkaḷukkum tevatāṉamāka iṟaiyiliyāka kuṭuttom nam molai pi-
ku kuṭu • • kuṭinīṅkāt tevatāṉamāka yaṉupavikka[vu*]m ippaṭik kalliluñ
cempilu[m] veṭṭik koḷka namata •••yuḷḷākaḷu • mākecuraru nokka

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 10 paṇams for a twilight lamp to be lighted in the temple of
Amaraviṭaṅgap Perumāḷ at Pāriyūr by Ūraṉ Periya Piḷḷaiyāṇḍaiyār of
maṉaiya vēṭṭuvar community and a native of Pāriyūr in Kāñchikkūval-nāḍu.
On accepting the money, the śiva-brāhmaṇas who had the right of service
(kaṇi) in the temple agreed to maintain the lamp.

l.ta: nāṭṭup pāriyūril [ma]ṉaiya veṭṭuva[ri]l ūraṉ periya piḷḷaiyāṇṭaiy-
yār amaraviṭaṅkap perumāḷ tirukkoyilukku cantiyātīpam oṉṟu eṉṉuta-
vatākavum itukku pattup paṇam ik koyilil kāṇiyuṭaiya civap

vinta paṭṭaṉum •••varum ippaṇam pattum upaiyamākak kaikkoṇṭu
muṭṭāmal na •••••

kavum ik ko •••ṭaṅkoṇṭu koyil pukuvāṉ yāvaṉoruvaṉ
cantirātittavarai ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the left side door-post of the western entrance into the maṇḍapa in
front of the central shrine in the Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records that the door-post was erected by Muḍikoṇḍa Chākāṇi,
nephew (marumagaṉ) of Mallayya Chākāṇi of chākāṇi community and a
native of Māmbaḷḷi in the Hoysaḷa country.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the right side door-post of the western entrance into the maṇḍapa in
front of the central shrine in the Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records that the door-post was erected by Ponnan Paṉaiyan of
paṉaiya vēṭṭuvar community of Pāriyūr.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1910.)
PĀRIYŪR (Pāriyūrgrāmam), GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK,
COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
On the base round the Nandi maṇḍapa in the courtyard of the
Amaraphaṇīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Kali 4966, Raktāṭchi, Paṅguni, 25, Wednesday, Pūsam = A.D. 1865, April 5,
Wednesday.).

Records that the Nandi maṇḍapa was constructed in the temple of
Amarapaṇīśvarasvāmi at Pāriyūr in Kāñchikūval-nāḍu by Kāḻiyaṇa Gouṇḍaṉ
of Kaṇvāḻ gōthram, a veḷḷāḷa of Nañjēgavuṇḍaṉ-pāḷaiyam.

l.ta:
yitiṉ mel cellāniṉṟa ṟaktāṭci varuṣam
paṅkuṉi mātam
teti puta vāṟamum tacamiyum pūca naṭca

tināma yokamum
tai tulākaṟaṇamum peṟṟa cupatiṉattil kāñcikūval nāṭṭil pāriyūriliru-

vupayam

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1910.)
KAḌAGATTŪR, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1352, Sādhāraṇa, Tai, śu., 1, Sunday, Tiruvōṇam = A.D. 1431, January
14, Sunday.).

Unfinished. Mentions Kaḍaikkoṭṭūr alias Dēsi Uyakoṇḍachōḻa-paṭṭaṇam
in Tagaḍa-nāḍu, a subdivision of Gaṅgai-nāḍu which was a district of
Nigarilichōḻa-maṇḍalam.
Published in KRMK., No. 122/1974, p. 177.

l.ta: coḻa maṇṭa[la]ttuk kaṅka nāṭṭut takaṭa nāṭṭu kaṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1910.)
KAḌAGATTŪR, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The Nagaram granted taxes on looms (taṟi-iṟai) in the
tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of Muḍigoṇḍa Chōḻīśvaramuḍaiya Nāyaṉār temple for
maintaining one perpetual lamp in the temple.
Published in KRMK., No. 125/1974, p. 179.

l.ta:
nāyanār muṭikoṇṭa coḷīcuramuṭaiya nāyanār tiruveḻicciyum
tiruccāntum nakaram yeḻuntaruḷuvik kaṭavom nakarattom muṭiko[ṇṭa]
coḷī curamuṭaiya nāyanār tirumaṭaiviḷākattil taṟi iṟai

yāl uḷḷatu nāyaṉārkkut tiru[viḷak]kukku viṭṭom cantirātittavarai
cellak kaṭavatāka viṭṭom nakarattom

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1910.)
KAḌAGATTŪR, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On the north and south walls of the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Śaka 1398, Dunmuki, Dhanu, śu., 8, Thursday, Uttira = A.D. 1476.)

Gift of certain pieces of land in favour of Kāḷatti Nāchchiyār and
other dancing women of the temple of Muḍikoṇḍachōḻichura at Kaḍaikāṭṭūr
in Tagaḍai-nāḍu.
Published in KRMK., Nos. 128-129/1974, pp. 181-182.

l.ta:
pati emmaṇṭalamum īḻamumuntiṟai koṇṭa
kecaveṭṭaikaṇṭa piṟatāpa

ruvāramum uttirattu nāḷ takaṭai nāṭṭu

vākūrile ērikīḻe

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1910.)
KAḌAGATTŪR, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the Chōḻīśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Śaka 1362, Dunmati, Vaikāsi, śu., 5, Sunday, Uttirāḍa (irregular) = A.D. 1440.)

Gift of certain taxes to the temple of Muḍigoṇḍa-Chōḻichuramuḍaiya
Nāyaṉār at Kaṭaikaṭṭūr alias Dēsi Uyyakkoṇḍachōḻa-paṭṭaṇa, in Tagaḍa-nāḍu
alias Gaṅga-nāḍu, a subdivision of Nigarilichōḻa-maṇḍalam by the Telugu
chief Mala Rāhuttar of Muḷvāych-chāvaḍi, while the Pradhāni Mādaṇa-
Daṇṇāyakkar of Muḷvāy was governing the country. The taxes gifted by
the donor are nāḍu-daḷavārikkai, cheṭṭiyār-magamai, and aḷḷāya-māṉiyam.
Published in KRMK., No. 121/1974, p. 175.

l.ta: ḷ nikarilicoḻa maṇṭalattuk kaṅka nāṭṭut takaṭa nāṭṭuk ka-
vum cantaiyaḷil oru kaṭai aṭikkācu aḷḷāya māṉiyamākac can-

kkaraiyiṟ kurāṟ pacuk koṉṟāṉ pāpaṅ koḷḷuvaṉ
§

p.b: No king. Dated in cyclic year Dundhubhi.

inscription.p:
Stipulates that the cultivators of lands below the tanks in the
Kaḍagattūr country must give śarivāram., i.e., equal share of the produce to
the owner and pay a specified fee for strengthening the tank bunds with
stone and earth. This was done for the merit of the chief Jagadēva.

l.te: duṁdubhi saṁvacra(tsara) phālgu(lgu)ṇa śu 10 nāḍu kaḍagatturi sīma ceruvula
kaṭaku rāya kaṭṭeṁduku kha 1 ki 81 lekhanu yetti ceruvula
ku vēyagalavāḍu yī nirṇayānaku yevaḍu tappi ceruvu kaṭaku
mannu rāya kaṭiṁ(ṭṭiṁ)ccakunnanu kāpu sarivāraṁ naḍapakunnanu
tama taliki tapina vāḍu talitaṁḍrini kāśilōna vadhiyiṁcina pāpā

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1910.)
DHARMAPURI, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On a granite pillar lying in the quarter known as Virupakshipuram.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. I, No. 23.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1910.)
DHARMAPURI, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On the fragment of a pillar lying in the house of Isvarayya.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. I, No. 38.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in front of the Aṅkāḷamma temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, pt. II, No. 532.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM
DISTRICT.
On the north and west bases of the Bhairava temple.

p.b: Narasiṅga II. Year 17: Kali 4386, Śaka 1[1]57 = A.D. 1235.

inscription.p:
Records the grant of three pieces of land at Nandimaṅgalam as a
temple kāṇi to Viḍukādar, the son of Śaivāchāri Periyadēvar by Paramaya-
Charakaṇi who built the temple of Paramēśvaramuḍaiyār at
Mahēndramaṅgalam from basement to finial and consecrated it. Of the
three pieces of land, one was a garden land and the other two pieces have
a sowing capacity of 18 and 3 kaṇḍukas each.
Published in KRMK., No. 18/1975, pp. 153-154.

l.ta:
ntamākac ceytu inta nāyaṉārukku nantimaṅkalattilum
nāṉ iṭṭa [ma]ṭattuk kaṭṭukkūliyāka nāṉ peṟṟuṭaiya nīrnilam melait
tūmpil mutal maṭaiyil patiṉaiṅ kaṇṭaka vitaiyum

ivverikku kīḻarukiṟ koyil nāyaṉār eḻuntaruḷi irukkiṟa toṭṭanilam
nāṟpālellaiyum it toṭṭattukkuk kīḻaruke mukkaṇṭaka vitai kaḻaṉiyum
tiruccūla

aivvācāriyap periya tevar makaṉ viṭukātaṟkku
ikkoyil kāṇiyāka nīrvārttuk kuṭuttu i

ttavarai
celvatāka kuṭutteṉ paramey carakaṇiyeṉ it taṉmattai
vilakkināruṇṭākil kaṅkaik karaiyi[ṟ] kurāṟ pacuvaik koṉṟāṉ
pā[vaṅ] koḷvāṉ vallavaraiyaṉ cattiyam

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM
DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the Bhairava temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 10 poṉ for a lamp by Maṅgalaṅ-kiḻavaṉ Nāgāṇḍai, to the
temple of Mayaṉadīchuram Uḍaiyār. Tillai Mūvāyiravan Nāḍāḻvāṉ and
Aṇukkanambi Periyanāyaṉ, the two śivabrāhmaṇas holding service right in
the temple agreed to maintain the grant.
Published in KRMK., No. 19/1975, p. 155.

l.ta: nāyaṟṟu uṭaiyār mayaṉatīcuram uṭaiyāṟku maṅkalaṅ kiḻa-

[vaṉ] nākāṇṭaiyen vaitta tirununtā viḷakku oṉṟukku upaiyamāka ik
koyiṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya

n nā[ṭā]ḻvāṉum aṇukkanampi periyanāyaṉum koṇṭa pon pattu
cantirātittavarai celvatem

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the Bhairava temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription, in three pieces, is unfinished and much damaged.
Seems to register a gift of land to the temple of Paramēchuram Uḍaiyār
for the welfare of Vīra Sōmēśvaradēva. Mentions Viḍukādar, the
śaivāchāri of Mayinadīramaṅgalam temple and Sōmaiya Daṇṇākkar.
I Piece

l.ta:
ttu vīracomīcuratevaṉ toḷḷukkum vāḷḷukkum naṉ[ṟā]kavum comaya
taṇṇākka-
kīḻviṭa nilam orumā piṭāripaṭṭikku kokku vāyakkālluṭpaṭa yaḷḷa-

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Bhairava temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 20 kaṇḍakam of land located below the tank of Kuḷattūr to
the temple of Mayindīchuramuḍaiya Nāyanār, by Sōmaya Daṇḍanāyaka
to perform a festival for the welfare of the king.
Published in KRMK., Nos. 22-26/1975, pp. 157-158.

l.ta:
curamuṭaiya nāyanārkkuk kumāra kaṇṭar kovan comaya taṇṭanāyakkanen
tevan tirumeṉikkuṉaṉṟāka tiruṉāḷ eḻuntaruḷa utakaṭpa
mākak kuḷattūr ēri kīḻt tūmpaṭi centa iṭattiley viṭṭa[vi]nilam
irupatiṉ kaṇṭakamum viṭṭen it tanma māṟṟuvā-

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On a slab lying in front of the Sōmēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. The Nāṭṭār of 79 Nāḍus and Nagarams in Tagaḍai-nāḍu,
Puṟamalai-nāḍu, and [Aṅga]ṇap-paṟṟu, remitted the tax kaṅka-kāṇi on
temples for the welfare of the king and Sōmaya Daṇṇākkar.
Published in KRMK., No. 33/1975, pp. 160-161.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Sōmēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Much damaged. A portion of imprecation is found at the end.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Sōmēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to record a grant of land as kuḍinīṅgā
dēvadāṉam to the temple of Mayindīchiram Uḍaiyār by a native of
Āriyambākkam. The inscription further states that the individual shall
measure out paddy at the rate of 60 kaṇḍakams on the produce of a kār
crop, and 10 kaṇḍakams on a picānam crop.

l.ta:
ṭṭen envaṅkicaramuḷḷatanaiyum naṭappattu • • ivane aḷakka kaṭava nellu
kārukku

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1910.)
ADHAMANKŌṬṬAI (Adhiyamāṉkōṭṭai), DHARMAPURI TALUK,
SALEM DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Sōmēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of gold for maintaining one perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mayindīśvaram Uḍaiyār, by a vāṇiyaṉ, Arumbākkiḻān Nāyan for the
welfare of his son Cāmāḻvān Pammāḻvāṉ. The money seems to have been
invested in buying a piece of land.

l.ta:
kku vāṇiyaṉ arumpāk kiḻā[n] nāyaṉen en makan cāmāḻvān
pammāḻvāṉuk-
ku meṉikkum naṉṟāka innāyaṉāṟku vaicca tirunantāviḷakku
m aṇukka nampi periyanāyaṉ ciṟunāyan poṉṉāḻvā • •-

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1910.)
MŌDŪR, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On a slab setup in the field called Chāmuṇḍi Ammaṉ Maṇḍu.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the remission of marriage tax (kaliyāṇa āyam) in
Tagaḍa-nāḍu, a subdivision of Nigarilichōḻa-maṇḍalam by Vīmappa
Nāyakkar, son of Bomma Nāyakkar, an officer ‘who possessed the strength
of the king Vishṇuvardhana’.

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1910.)
MŌDŪR, DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On a boulder in front of the temple of Nelapperumāḷ.
inscription.p: (Aṅgirasa, Māsi, 17 (not verifiable) = A.D. 1152, February (9, Saturday.).

Records the oral order of the king granting a portion of land with all
trees and wells at Mōdūr in Tagaḍa-nāḍu, a district of Nigarilichōḻa-
maṇḍalam, as sarvamānyam to the deity Siṅgapperumāḷ, installed in the
temple of Durgaiyār in Padi-nāḍu, a district of Mēl-maṇḍala. The royal
order was issued by the king while he was on a visit to the place
[Gōvinda] Paṭṭaṇam. The royal official Rājarāja Brahmarāyaṉ attested the
document.
Published in KRMK., No. 80/2005, p. 249.

l.ta: kku yāṇṭu mūṉṟāvatu āṅkira varuṣattu māci mātam
nāḷ nikarilicoḻa maṇṭalattu kaliṅkaṟaip paṟṟil maḷi-
pati nāṭṭil śrīmaṟ tuṟkaiyār ko[yi]lil nāyaṉār ciṅkapperumāḷukku
nikarilicoḻa maṇṭalattu takaṭa nāṭṭil motūr tevatāṉam tiruvi-
ṭaiyāṭṭa nīkki iṉṉāḷ mutal nañcai puñcai nālppālellaikkuḷppaṭṭa
menokkiṉa maramum kīḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟum maṟṟum eppeṟppaṭṭaṉa-

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1910.)
OḌḌAPPAṬṬI, HARUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On a hero-stone setup near a well.
inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters and engraved with inter-lineation. Records that
Kavikalināḍaiya Erumaiya-nakkar, a servant of Teḷiniyār, fell while the
latter captured Yeruvāyil.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1910.)
OḌḌAPPAṬṬI, HARUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On another hero-stone setup near a well.
inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. The inscription is engraved around the images
of two individuals holding small daggers and long bows in their hands.
Puḷḷis are marked in some places. Records that two persons namely,
Erumaiya Eruvāḍi and Kuṉṟandai Vaḍugaṉ fell while lifting cattle (toṟuk-
koṇḍa-ñāṉṟu) when Māṟavoṭṭaiyār was ruling Tāyaṉūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1910.)
KALLAVI, HARUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
On a boulder near the 157th mile stone on the Railway line.

p.b: No king or date. (15th cent. A.D.).

inscription.p:
Records the erection of liṅgamudre stone and construction of a maṭha
for Karibasavadēva of Hiratta-maṭha (Virakta-maṭha) by Paravotamuḍeya.

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The name of the king is lost. Nithyānantha Bhaṭṭan, a śiva-brāhmaṇa
figures in the following inscription (No. 216), which is dated in the reign
of Rājarāja III. Therefore this inscription could be assigned to Rājarāja III.

Records that Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ, a brāhmaṇa of Tiruveṇkāḍu
in Nāṅgūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Chōḻa-maṇḍalam, installed the deity
Aḻagiya Tiruchchiṟṟambalam Uḍaiya Nāyaṉār in the temple of
Tiruvallidāyamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār at Tiruvallidāyam in Ambattūr-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam alias Vikramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, in
Jayangoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam and granted ten aṉṟādu-naṟ-paḻaṅkācu for
providing services, sacred food and for lighting one twilight lamp (chandi
viḷakku). The ten kācus were received by the three śiva-brāhmaṇas
holiding the right of service (kāṇi) in the temple as deposit and agreed to
provide the above services. Mentions the grain measure tiruñānsambanda-
nāḻi.

l.ta: ṟukku uḷḷuc coḻa maṇṭalattu nāṅkūr nāṭṭut ti-
yile tiruñāṉacampanta nāḻiyāle iru nāḻi ariciyum
amutum amutu ceyvittukkoḷḷak kaṭavomākavum oru canti
viḷakku erikkak kaṭavomākac cammatittom ik koyiṟ tiruvuṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Plavaṅga, Mithuna, śu., 12, Friday, Anilam (Anusham) = A.D. 1427, June 6,
Friday.).

A sarvamānya gift of tax, (iḍaṅgai vari) was made in favour of the
temple of Tiruvallidāyam in Ambattūr-nāḍu in Puḻal-kōṭṭam alias
Vikramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, a subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The
tax, was levied on the iḍaṅgai classes and vaṉṉiyars from the many
paṟṟus of the Chandragiri region. The income from the tax shall be
utilized for conducting the hunting (tiruvēṭṭai) festival in the temple. The
record was signed by Iḍaṅgai Mīkāmavēḷāṉ.

l.ta: ku cantirakiri cūḻnta pala paṟṟil iṭaṅkaiyi-
m paḷḷikaḷ peril iṭaṅkai vari uḷḷatukku
ykāppāṟku nāṭṭil oṉṟu pātiyum kuṭikku oṉṟu mukkā-
lum koḷḷak kaṭavarkaḷākavum ippaṭi cammatittu kalve-
cantirakiri cūḻnta pala paṟṟil iṭaṅkaiyil uḷḷārum
kuṭuttom ippaṭikku ivai iṭaṅkai mīkāmaveḷāṉ eḻuttu •

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Māmbākkiḻāṉ installed an image of Kshētrapālap Piḷḷaiyār in the
temple of Tiruvallidāyamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār at Tiruvallidāyam of Ambattūr-
nāḍu, in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam, a subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, and
gifted 5 aṉṟāḍu-naṟ-paḻaṅkācu for providing one twilight lamp (chandhi
viḷakku) and food offerings to the deity. The money was received by the
śiva-brāhmaṇas, who agreed to maintain the lamp.

l.ta:
vatu
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattup puḻaṟ koṭṭattampattūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār
tiruvalli tāyamu-

uṭaiyāṉ māmpākkiḻāṉ perumāḷ iṉṉāyaṉār koyilukku ivar

ceyvittuk koḷḷak kaṭavomāka ivar pakkal nāṅkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa
aṉṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅkācu

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Makara, śu., 4, Sunday, Uttiraṭṭādi = A.D. 1253, January 5, Sunday.)

Records an inventoy of gold and silver ornaments received by the
four officials of the temple of Tiruvallidāyam in Ambattūr-nāḍu of Puḻal-
kōṭṭam, a subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The ornaments were
presented to the image Tiruvīdi Nāchchiyār and also to the presiding deity
of the temple by Chōḻappiḷḷaiyār Nāchchiyār, a dēvaraḍiyāḷ of the temple
of Tiruvēgambamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār at Kāñchīpuram. The above image was
installed by Chōḻappiḷḷaiyār Nāchchiyār in memory of her mother
Thaṅkachchiyār.

l.ta:
tiyum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
uttiraṭṭāti nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu eyiṟ ko-

ṭṭattu nakaraṅ kāñcipurattu uṭaiyār tiruvekampamuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil •
• • [yakap] perunteruvil tevaraṭiyāril taṅkacciyār makaḷā[r*]

nāṭṭu tiru-

mātoṭuṟaikiṉṟa uṭaiyār tiruvallitāyamuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkku taṅkacciyār

nācciyāṟkkum uṭaiyāṟkkum cāttiṉa tiruvāparaṇaṅkaḷ paṭṭam
oṉṟuṉāl poṉ irukaḻañce mukkālum tiruviḷampiṟai

poṉ kaḻañcum uṭpuṟattu iṭṭa veḷ[ḷi*] kaḻañcu nācciyārkku cāttiṉa
tirumaṅkali nāṇ kaḻañcum tiricaram oṉṟiṉāl pata muttu eṭṭum itiṉ
kīḻttaram muttu aññūṟṟu irupattu eṭṭum poṉmaṇi iraṇṭum niṇṭāyap
pa-

vaḻam patiṉmūṉṟu kuṇṭaip pavaḻam nāṟpatum āka eṇpatalāṉa iṭai patiṉ

kum caṅkiṟ kālum tirumuttuvāḷi iraṇṭuṉāl muttup patiṉāṟu itukku

tiruppaḷḷiaṟai nācciyāṟkum tiruvīti nācciyāṟku-

m tirukkāṟcarikku iṭṭa veḷḷi irupatiṉ kaḻañcu uṭaiyāṟkuc cāttiṉa
keṅkā tevi nācciyāṟku veḷḷi eḻukaḻañcu ikkoyil tevaraṭiyāril
poṉṉāṇṭai iṭṭa paṭṭam
paṭṭaṉum kulottuṅka paṭṭaṉum ivvaṉaivom itu paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Built in at the beginning. Seems to refer an old grant of 1200 kuḻis
of land as dēvadāṉa in Chintāmaṇipuram to the temple of
Tiruvallidāyamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār by Nāḍāḻvāṉ of Chūriyaṉ Paiyyūr. These
lands were now regranted by Saḷukki Nārāṇan Yādavarāyan.

l.ta:
•••ppuḻaṟ koṭṭamāṉa vikkiracoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu ampattūr nāṭṭu
tiruvallitāyattil piṟi-

viṭṭeṉ

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 3 aṉṟāḍu-naṟ-paḻaṅkācu for one twilight lamp (chandhi
viḷakku) by Chilambaṇindāṉ Yādavarāyaṉ of Paramēchuramaṅgalam in
Chembūr-kōṭṭam to the temple of Tiruvallidāyamuḍaiya Nāyanār in Puḻal-
kōṭṭam. The money was received by śiva-brāhmaṇas of the temple who
agreed to maintain the lamp.

l.ta:
nāyanāṟku cempūṟ koṭṭattu paramecuramaṅkalamuṭaiyān cilampaṇintāṉ
yātavarāyaṉ [paccira]mpāṇṭār innā-

yanāṟku cantirātittavarai celvatāka vaitta cantiviḷakkonṟu ivviḷakku
onṟum ikkoyilil civappirāma-

kottirattu cikaṇṭanāta paṭṭan uḷḷiṭṭārum

kaikkoṇṭa anṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅkācu mūṉṟu ippaḻaṅkācu mūṉṟukkum
cantiviḷakkoṉṟum cantirātittavarai erippatākak kaik-

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Tiruvallīśvara
temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1548 (wrong), Nandana, Mēsham, śu., 7, Wednesday, Pūsam = A.D.
1472, April 15, Wednesday.)

Assignment of 21 chōḍi paṇam from Irāchāchiraiya-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
alias Agaram Kuṟaṭṭūr as a sarvamānya gift of Sāḷuva Bommaiyadēva
Mahārāja, for maintaining five lamps in the temple of Pāḍi Tiruvallidāyam
in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam alias Ambattūr-nāḍu in Vikramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, a
subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: yum putaṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūcattu nāḷ

jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu vikkiṟamacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu puḻaṟ koṭṭamāṉa
ampattūr nāṭṭu pāṭi tiruvallitāyattil uṭaiyār tiruvallitāyamuṭaiya
nāyiṉāṟku irācāciṟaiya catu

teṟkil akaram vaṭakkil akarattil makājaṉam muṉṉāḷ nāyiṉār
tiruvallitāyamuṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku viṭṭa tiruviḷakkuppaṭikku naṭakkiṟa coṭi
paṇam irupattu oṉṟum

ya nāyiṉār tirumuṉpe tiruṉuntāviḷakkāka niṟṟam nālu tiruviḷakkum

m pacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile pokak kaṭava-

inscription.head:
No. 220A.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Tiruvallīśvara
temple.

p.b: No king or date. (15th cent. A.D.).

inscription.p:
In two fragments. Seems to refer to some grant to the temple. The
purport of the grant is not clear.
I Piece

koṭai tirupa •••••••••• kāṇiāṭci cantirātittavarai

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north and west walls of the central shrine in the Tiruvallīśvara
temple.
inscription.p: (Dhātri, Makara, śu., 10, Uttiram (irregular) = A.D. 1397.).

The taxes paṭṭaḍai-āyam, paṭṭaḍai-nūlāyam and other taxes levied on
the cheṭṭis, kaikkōḷas, vāṇigar and on those people working in the eighteen
professional work-shops (patineṭṭup-paṭṭaḍai) in Pāḍi-Tiruvallidāyam were
assigned to the temple of Tiruvallidāyamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār, in Ambattūr-nāḍu,
of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam alias Vikramachōḻa vaḷanāḍu, a subdivision of
Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, by an order of Mallaṇṇa-Uḍaiyār.

l.ta:
ṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭāṉ koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ
pā

kkut tappuvār irāyar kaṇṭaṉ śrī vīraariyarirāyaṉukkuc cellā
niṉṟa tātri varuṣattu makaranāyaṟṟup pūṟuvapakṣattu-

t tecamiyum peṟṟa uttirattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattup puḻaṟ
koṭṭam āṉa vikkiṟamacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu ampattūr nāṭṭil pāṭi
tiruvallitāyattil uṭaiyār tiruvallitā-

yamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku mallaṇṇa uṭaiyā[r*] nirupapaṭikkup pāṭi
tiruvallitāyattil paṭṭaṭaikkuṭi ceṭṭikaḷ kaikkoḷar vāṇikar eṇṇai
vāṇikar maṟṟum uḷḷiṭṭa

āyattai nokki varum ilāpalokaṅkaḷkkum

varaikkum ca

māṉiyam āka naṭakkakaṭavatuākac cilālekai paṇṇik
kuṭutteṉ ittaṉmattukku akitam niṉaittār uṇṭākil keṅkai
karaiyiṟk kurāp pacuvaik koṉṟa-

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Madurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa mentioned in this inscription could be
identified with one of the feudatories of Rājarāja III and the year 33 cited
in the inscription perhaps be the regnal year of his overlord (Rājarāja III).

Records an order of Madurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa to the Ūrār
granting the income from the village (with the exclusion of old grants
assigned as dēvadāṉa iṟaiyili) of Tiruvallidāyam for undertaking repair in
the temple and for feeding the māhēshvaras. The inscription ends with the
signature of Gaṇḍagōpala.

l.ta:
maturāntaka pottappiccoḻaṉ Olai jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu
puḻalk koṭṭattu am-
pattūr nāṭṭu tiruvallitāyattu [ū]ravar kaṇṭu taṅkaḷūr iṟaiyili

tu mūṉṟāvatu mutal uṭaiyār tiruvallitāyamuṭaiya nā[ya*]ṉāṟku
tiruppaṇikkup pātiyum

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Grant of tax free land measuring 400 kuḻis to the temple of
Tiruvallidāyam in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam for maintaining two twilight lamps (chandhi
viḷakku) by two individuals, namely Tirumalaiyuḍiayāṉ and Uyyavandāṉ.
The land was earlier purchased from the dēvadāṉa village in a public
auction for a consideration of 3 aṉṟāḍu-naṟ-paḻankācu. The śivabrāhmaṇs
accepted the money and agreed to maintain the lamp.

l.ta:
vatu puḻaṟ
koṭṭattu uṭaiyār tiruvallitāyamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku ikkoṭṭattu
tirumaṅ-

kalaṅkiḻār kaṉmikaḷil kāraikiḻāṉ tirumalaiyuṭaiyāṉum ivūrilyirukkum
veḷḷāḷaṉ uyyavantāṉum iṉṉāyaṉār tevatāṉamāṉa pāṭiyilyiṉ-

ṉāyaṉār tirunāmakkāṇiyāṉa nilattil ērivāyil añcupaṭṭi paṟṟil
vaṭakkil taṭiyiraṇṭil kiḻakku taṭi oṉṟināl kuḻi nāṉūṟum iṟaiyiḻicci
vilai aṉṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅ-

vilaiyākak
koṇṭu iṉṉāyaṉārkku cantirātittavaraiyiraṇṭu cantiviḷakku
erippatākak koṇṭu vi-

kaik koṇṭu cantiviḷakku iraṇṭum cantirātittavarai erikka kaṭavarkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Vikāri, Siṅgha, śu., 10, Monday, Anuttu (Anusham) = A.D. 1419, September,
31, Monday, Jyēshṭa.)

Damaged and incomplete. Assignment of kāṇi right of the village
Niyāyaparipālaṉ-pēṭṭai to the temple of Tiruvallidāyamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār at
Tiruvallidāyam in Ambattūr-nāḍu in Puḻal-kōṭṭam alias Vikramachōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu, a subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam for a consideration of
180 vāsipaḍā-naṟ-paṇam by four persons holding the kāṇi right of the
village. It was stipulated that the tax and other dues to the State shall be
paid by the seller of the kāṇi.

l.ta:
kkut tappuvarāyār kaṇṭaṉ mūvarāyar
kaṇṭaṉ pū

camiyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa aṉuttu
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu puḻa[l*] koṭṭam āṉa vikkiṟamacoḻa
vaḷaṉāṭṭu ampattūr nāṭṭu tiruvallitāyattu ṉāyaṉār tiruva-

llitāyam uṭai[ya*]ṉāyaṉār śrīpaṇṭārattu imaṇṭalattu ik koṭṭattu
iṉṉāṭṭu vicaiyālaiya nallūrāṉa nuḷappiyāṟṟu nuḷappiyāṟṟuḻār
irācarāca vi[ḻu]pparaiyar uḷḷiṭṭārum meṟpaṭi koṅkarācar uḷḷi-

ṭṭārum meṟpaṭi periyaṉāyaṉār uḷḷiṭṭārum meṟpaṭi villavarāyārum
ivvaṉaivarom kāṇivilai piṟamā[ṇa*]m paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi eṅka(ṅ)ḷ
kāṇiāṉa nuḷappiyāṟṟu maṇañceri āṉa niyāyaparipāla-

ṉ peṭṭai kīḻpāṟkku ellai pāṭiyil melait tiruvītikku meṟkum mel
pāṟ kellai pāṭiyil melait tiruviti[k*]ku[m*] meṟkum [meṟkum]
melp[ā*]ṟ kellaip paṉaccaik kuḻiyil kīḻaikkālukku meṟkkum
teṉpā-

ṟ teṉpāṟkellai āccapirāṉ vaṭapālaicceṟu maṇali vaṭakku taricukku
vaṭakkum vaṭapāṟkellai puṉṉaivāyalāṉ kālukku teṟkum iṉṉāṉku
ellaikku uṭppaṭṭa nañcai nilamum

puṉcai nilamum nattamum nattal maṉaiyum maṉaipaṭappum meṉṉokkiṉa maramu
kīḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟum kaṉṟumeypāḻum kāṭum piṭaliyum kuḻiyum teṭṭamum
nīrum nīroṭukālum maṟṟum eppeṟppaṭṭac cakalaāṟattikkum vilai āka
emmil icaintu vilaipporuḷ aṉṟāṭaṉ naṭakkum vāci-

ippaṇam nūṟṟueṇpatu ippaṇam nūṟṟueṇpatukkum
ivvūr vilai āvatu ākavum ivvūṟku eppeṟppaṭṭa kalaṉum illai
kalaṉuḷḷavai toṟṟuppaṭilṉāṅkaḷe kalaṉ tīrttu tarakkaṭavom ākavum
ivvūr ivaṟku viṟṟu oṟṟi ātitātiṉmaṅkaḷukku maṟṟu eṟppeṟ-

ppaṭṭac caka[la]āṟttikaḷukku urittuāka kaṭavatu ākavum ippaṇam
nūṟṟueṇpatukkum ituve poruḷmāvaṟutip poruḷccelavolai āvatu
ākavum itu allatu veṟu poruḷmāvaṟutip poruḷccelavolai kāṭṭu

eṟṟameṉṟu collappeṟātākavum ivvūrkku ellaic cīrmaika[ḷi]le
tiruccūla-

param paṇṇikkoḷḷakkaṭavarkaḷ ākavum ivvūrkku iṟaiiṟuppum
irācakarattukkum cantirātittavaraikkumṉāṅkaḷe iṟuttuk koḷḷak
kaṭavomākavum ippiramāṇattukku Olaikkuṟṟam eḻuttup piḻai vācakap
paḻutu vāṭṭueṟṟam eṉṟu collap peṟātākavum iccātaṉappaṭikku

irukālāvatu mukkālāvatu ippaṇam nūṟṟueṇpatukkum ivvūr vilaikkuṟa
viṟṟup poruḷuṟakaikkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilaippiṟamāṇam
paṇṇikkoṭuttomṉāyaṉār tiruvallitāyammuṭaiyaṉāyaṉārkku
ca

kuṭuttom ippaṭikku nuḷappiyāṟṟuḻāṉ irā[ca*]rāca
viḻupparaiyaruḷḷiṭṭārum meṟpaṭi koṅkarāyar uḷḷiṭṭārum meṟpaṭi
periyaṉāyaṉār uḷḷiṭṭārum meṟpaṭi villavarāyar uḷḷiṭṭārum ivva-

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On a slab lying in the courtyard of the Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The assembly of Kuṟaṭṭūr in Ambattūr-ērikīḻ-nāḍu, a subdivision of
Puḻal-kōṭṭam sold a tax free land measuring 1,350 kuḻis, situated on the
southern part of the village Kuṟaṭṭūr for lighting a lamp in the temple of
Mahādēva at Tiruvalidāyil in Tuḍamuni-nā[ḍu], a subdivision of Puliyūrk-
kōṭṭam. The temple priests agreed to maintain the perpetual lamp. The
document was written by the scribe as per the instructions of the sabhā.
Mentions the land measuring rod patiṉaṟu-cāṉ-kōl.

l.ta: ṭa nilam patiṉaṟucāṇ kolāl āyirattu muṉnūṟṟaim-
patu kuḻiyum itupeṟu vilaikāṇamaṟak koṇṭu tiruvalitāyat-
lamoḻiviṉṟi yitu peṟu vilaikkāṇamaṟak kuṭuttu viṟṟukkoṇ[ṭa]-
sana mutalākat tām veṇṭukovukke nicati kaḻañcu ponṟaṇṭa-
ḷ koviṉāl vanta vikkamuḷḷatu tīrttuk kuṭuppomāṉom
vūrp peruṅkiḻavaṉ niṉṟai nūṟṟeṇma paṭṭaṉeṉ ippaṭi
aṟiveṉ tiruvallitāyil kūṭṭattāṉ naraciṅka paṭṭaṉeḻuttu ip-
ṉṉeṉ ippaṭiyaṟiveṉ koṉṟūrk kūṭṭattāṉ tivākara pa-

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the same slab lying in the courtyard of the Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to the temple of Mahādēva at
Tiruvalidāyil in Tuḍamuṇiyūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Puliyūrk-kōṭṭam by
Palayaṉ Tāḻi Ayaṇakkāḷi, a śaṅkarappāḍi from Aṇithirappāḍi.

l.ta: tirumakaḷ polap perunilac celviyun taṉakke urimai pū-
nāṭuṅ kaṅpāṭiyu nuḷampa pāṭiyun taṭiya pāṭiyuṅ
llā yāṇṭun toḻuteḻa viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ce-
vatu puliyūrk koṭṭattu tuṭamuṇiyūr nāṭṭu tiruvalitāyi-
ḻi ayaṇakkāḷi vaitta cāvāmūvāp perāṭu toṇṇūṟu itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1910.)
PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the same slab lying in the courtyard of the Tiruvallīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. Gift of 120 kāḍi of paddy for sacred food
offerings to the deity Umā, consecrated by Cheṅgalaṅ-kiḻān
Tiruveḷikāṇippaṭṭan in the Mahādēva temple at Tiruvalidāyil in Tuḍamuṇi-
nāḍu, a subdivision of Puliyūrk-kōṭṭam. The priests of the central shrine of
the temple accepted the paddy as deposit and agreed to maintain the grant.

l.ta: ṇṭiṟal veṉṟit taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa taṉṉeḻil vaḷar ūḻiyuḷ e-
ḻuteḻa viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻiyaraic tecu koḷ-
ṇāḻikai uṭaiyoṅ kaiyyeḻuttu ivvūr ceṅkaḻaṅkiḻān ti-
nūṟṟarupatiṉ kāṭi innellukku palicaiyāka ivar eḻuntaruḷuvitta umā-
••••• ttu iru nāḻi ••••• t tiruvamutu cantirātittavaṟ celva •••••

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1910.)
CONJEEVERAM, CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south base of the Jvaraharēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Dhanu, śu., 1, Monday and Pūrāḍam = A.D. 1270, December 15,
Monday.).

The Pallava chief Tipurāsar Nallasittarasaṉ of Ambalūr granted income
from the tax on looms (taṟik-kaḍamai) and other taxes as sarvamāṉyam for
providing sacred food offerings and for lighting lamps in the temple of
Suravaṭṭāramuḍaiya Nāyaṉār in the city of Kāñchipuram in Eyiṟ-kōṭṭam, a
subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
maiyun
tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūrāṭattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu

eyiṟ koṭṭattu nakaraṅ kāñcipurattu uṭaiyār curavaṭṭāramuṭaiya
nāyaṉārkkup pallavava

ttu ampalūrt tipurācar nallacittaracaṉeṉ
iṉṉāyaṉār tirumaṭaiviḷākattil uḷḷa

taṟik kaṭamaiyum ittaṟikku varum epperpaṭṭa upātikaḷum iṉṉāyaṉārkku
amutupaṭi tiruviḷakkut tiruppaṇikku uṭalākac cantirātittavarai

yta ittaṉmattukku aḻivu ceytavarkaḷ keṅkaiyiṭaik kumariyiṭaik
kurāṟpacuvaik koṉṟāṉ pāvaṅ koḷḷakkaṭavarkaḷ ippaṭikku ivai
nallacittaracaṉ eḻuttu-

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 229 of 1910.)
CONJEEVERAM, CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Jvaraharēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Assignment of 1600 kuḻis of land by Sundarachōḻaṉ, a kiḻāṉ of
Vaḻashāṟu alias Kunivallanallūr of Viṟpēḍu-nāḍu, in Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam, a
subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, to the Suravaṭṭālamuḍaiya
Nāyaṉār in the city of Kāñchipuram in Eyiṟ-kōṭṭam, for services in the
temple as per a royal order issued by the king. Mentions a land measuring
rod padinālaḍik-kōl.

l.ta:
añcāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu eyiṟ koṭṭattu nakaraṅ
kāñcipurattu uṭaiyār curavaṭṭā-

lamuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkku nitta nimatañ celvatāka im maṇṭalattuk kāliyūr
koṭṭattu viṟpeṭu nāṭṭu va[ḻa]-

ṣāṟāṉa kunivalla nallūr teṟkkil ēriyil cuntaracoḻaṉ maṇṭapattu
eḻuntaruḷi iruntu tiruvāymoḻi-

ñ ceyta tirumukappaṭi ivvūr viṣāṟu kiḻān
cuntaracoḻaṉeṉ i[ṉ]ṉāyaṉārku

ivvūr teṉ kaḻani mala •••••••••• vatikku kiḻakku [meṟ]ku oṉṟukku taṭi
iraṇṭukku varukiṟa kālukku

meṟkum vaṭapāṟkellai ceṅkeṇi kālukkut teṟkum teṉpāṟkellai
viṟpeṭṭu ūr kālukkum kumaravatikkum

vaṭakku iṉṉāṟpāṟkellaikkum uṭpaṭa taṭi añciṉāl
patinālaṭikkolāl kuḻi āyirattu aṟunūṟum iṉṉāyaṉāṟ-
ku nitta nimantañ ce[y*]vatāka viṭṭom nāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1910.)
CONJEEVERAM, CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Jvaraharēśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Kīlaka, Kaṟkaṭa, śu., 5, Friday, Anilam (irregular) = A.D. 1369).

Incomplete. The māhēśvaras and tānattār of the temple of
Suravaḍinda Nāya[ṉa]r assigned the privilege of supervising the services of
the temple to Aḻagiya Tiruch-chiṟṟambalamuḍaiyār, the son of
Bhuvanēkabāku Dēvar.

l.ta:
miyum veḷḷikkiḻa[m]aiyum peṟṟa aniḻattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa
maṇṭalattu nakaraṅ kāñci-

rarum tānattārum tevar
puvaneka pākutevar makanār

aḻakiya tirucciṟṟampalam uṭaiyār[kku] palamutal pūcaikkaṇkāṇi[ya]m
iṉṉalaikku iṉṉāyanār koyil tiruppuṟak-

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1910.)
IḶAMBAYAṄGŌṬṬŪR, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south base of the temple of Dēvanāyakasvāmi.
inscription.p:
The inscription records that the stone temple (kaṟṟaḷi) was built for
Sivapādasēkhara Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of the village Kuṇukkarumpu by
Mārkkaṇḍaiyan alias Kūḻappiḍāraṉ, a sthapati from Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

l.ta:
panniraṇṭāvatu ittirukkaṟṟaḷi
ceyvittāṉ kuṇukkarumpāttup paṇṭāri kiḻāṉ civacaraṇālaiya-

nāṉa civapātacekara mūventaveḷāṉ ivaṟkkākac ceyvittāṉ
tiruvālaṅkāṭṭil mārkkaṇṭaiyanāna kūḻappiṭāraṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1910.)
IḶAMBAYAṄGŌṬṬŪR, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south base of the temple of Dēvanāyakasvāmi.
inscription.p:
Gift of 11 kaḻañcus of gold for lighting one perpetual lamp in the
temple at Iḷambayaṅkōṭṭūr in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Periyāḻvāṉ Vēyiḍaṅkoṇḍār, a kiḻāṉ of
Vallam in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The śiva-
brāhmaṇas of the temple accepted the money and agreed to maintain the
lamp.

l.ta:
immaṇṭalattu puliyūr koṭṭamāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu vallaṅ
kiḻā-

ippoṉ patiṉoru kaḻañcum kaikkoṇṭu ittirunaṉtāviḷakku
naṉṟāka-

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1910.)
IḶAMBAYAṄGŌṬṬŪR, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south base of the temple of Dēvanāyakasvāmi.
inscription.p:
A dēvadāṉa gift of lands at Kaḷiyanallūr in Tiruvēḷūr-nāḍu to the
temple of Iḷambayaṅkōṭṭūr, for the supply of sacred food offerings and
repairs by Madurāntaka Pottappichchōḻaṉ Vīra Gaṇḍagōpālaṉ.

l.ta: ṭṭār kāṇiya • • tiruveḷūr nāṭṭu kaḷiyanallūr nāṟpā-
ṟ e[l*]laikkum uṭpaṭṭa nīrnilattilum kollai nilattilum uḷḷūr
iṟaiyili t[e*]vatāṉa nīkki nīkkiniṉṟa nīrnilamum kollai nilamum
nāyanāṉāṟku amutum cāttuppaṭikkum tiruppaṇikkum uṭalāka aṉaittāya-
ṅkaḷum uṭpaṭat tevatāṉamāka viṭṭeṉ maturāntakap pottappicco-

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1910.)
IḶAMBAYAṄGŌṬṬŪR, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the west base of the temple of Dēvanāyakasvāmi.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and built in. Seems to refer to an earlier assignment of
land as tirunāmattukkāṇi by Sambuvarājaṉ to the sabhā of Kōṭṭūr alias
Chōḻavichchādira-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of
Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The land, measuring an
extent of 11(1/4) (vēlis) was sold for a consideration of 200 paḻaṅ-kācus.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravarttikaḷ vīrāvapiṣekamum vijaiyāvapiṣekamum
paṇṇiyaruḷiya śrī tiripuvaṉa vīratevaṟku yāṇṭu

sattu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattuk kaṉṟūr nāṭṭu
koṭṭūrāṉa coḻaviccātirac caturvetimaṅkalattu uṭaiyār
tiruvilampayaṅko-

rarom iṉṉāyanāṟku
muṉṉāḷile ceṅkeṇiyammaiyappaṉ vaṉṉiyaṉāyaṉ campuvarājaṉ ivvūṟ
sapaiyār pakkal tirunāmat-

tukkāṇiyākak koṇṭu variyiliṭṭa nilamāvatu ūruṇi tū •••••••••• ta
vatikku vaṭakku ērikaraikkuk kiḻakkuk kammāṉ koṭṭur ellaikkut teṟku
naṭuvupaṭṭa nilam patinoṉṟekā-

lukku mivarkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu irunūṟu ippa •••••••••• ṉṉilam
paṇṭāṭu paḻanaṭaiye ivvūṟ

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1910.)
IḶAMBAYAṄGŌṬṬŪR, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north base of the temple of Dēvanāyakasvāmi.
inscription.p:
Gift of one māḍai for a twilight lamp (chandhi viḷakku) to the temple
of Tiruviḷambamkōṭṭur-uḍaiya Nāyanar at Kōṭṭur alias Chōḻavichchādirach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṉaviṟ-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam by Śrī Āḍavallār, the daughter of Takkanāchchi,
a dēvaraḍiyār in the temple. The śiva-brāhmaṇas of the temple who
received the money undertook to maintain the lamp.

l.ta:
puvanaccakkaravattikaḷ maturai koṇṭu pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalai
koṇṭu vīrāapiṣekamum vicaiyāapiṣekamum paṇṇiaruḷiya

vaṟkku yāṇṭu muppattu nālāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa
maṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattuk kaṉṟūr nā(ā)-

ṭṭu koṭṭūrāṉa [coḷa coḻaviccātirac caturve]ti maṅkalattu uṭaiyār
tiruvilampamkoṭṭūruṭaiya nāya[ṉ]ār koyiṟ civappirāmaṇaromik koyiṟ
tevaraṭiyāril takkanācci makaḷ śrīāṭavallar vaiytta cantiviḷakku
oṉṟukku kaik ko-

ṇṭa māṭai oṉṟum kaikkoṇṭu oru canti eḻarai nāḻikai tiruviḷakku
erikka kaṭavomākac cammatittuk kaikkoṇṭom ikkoyil
civappirāmaṇarom itu

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1910.)
IḶAMBAYAṄGŌṬṬŪR, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north base of the temple of Dēvanāyakasvāmi.
inscription.p:
Gift of 5 cows for a twilight lamp (chandhi viḷakku) to be lighted in
the temple of Tiru-Iḷambayaṅkōṭṭūr in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of
Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam by Kulōttuṅgachōḻach-
chambuvarāyaṉ alias Aḻagiyachōḻaṉ. Two groups of brāhmaṇas belonging to
the cow shed (gōmaḍam) of the temple received the cows and agreed to
maintain the lamp for 15 days each.

l.ta: tu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattu kaṉṟūr nāṭṭu ā-

ḷuṭaiyār tiruilampayaṉkoṭṭur uṭaiya nāyaṉārkuk kulottuṅkacoḻac
cam-

puvarāyaṉ āṉa vaḻakiyacoḻaṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku viḷakku oṉṟukku
viṭṭa pacu

muppatu vaṭṭattu kaikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai ce ••••• kak
kaikkoṇṭom itu

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., 5, 880.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p: (Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Mēsham, ba. 3, Friday, Anusham = A.D. 1533.)

Gift of the village of Periya Puḷiyavi, surnamed Narasimhapuram and
a part of Kūvam alias Thyāgasamudranallur (Narasānāyakkapuram) in
Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Teṉkarai Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu, for the merit of the king by Tittāra-piḷḷai, on the occasion of the
consecration of the image of Lakshmī-Narasimhamūrti in the temple. The
lands in the village were divided into 29 shares and distributed among the
brāhmaṇas, talaiyāri, and sthānaththār of the temple.

l.ta:
koṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu teṉkarai maṇaviṟ koṭṭattu kaṉṟūr
ṉāṭṭil kūvamāna tiyāka

paṅkiraṇṭu •••••••••• ituvum voppa

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1457, Manmatha, Simham, śu., 12, Wednesday, Tiruvōṇam = A.D. 1535,
August 11, Wednesday.)

The inscription is partly damaged and incomplete. Gift of land and
house sites to six (?) śrīvaishṇava-brāhmaṇas of Narasānāyakapuram by
Tītta[ra*] Piḷḷai of Kāñchipuram, the secretary of the king,
Achchyutadēvarāya for the merit of Periyasvāmi Narasā Nāyakar. The
grant was instituted on the occasion of the consecration of the processional
image (utsavabhēra) of the presiding deity with the name, Prahalādha-
purandara in the Lakshminārāsimha Perumāḷ temple at Kūvam also known
as Tyāgasamudranallūr, in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Teṉkarai Maṇavil-
kōṭṭam in Chandragiri-rājya, of Ja[ya]ṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
ttu teṉkarai maṇavil
koṭṭattu kaṉṟūr ṉāṭṭuk kūvam

r appaiyaṅkāṟku vaṭakkut teru vītiyil meṟku mutal
mūṉṟu-

vaṉamātevi
viḷākattil ce[ṟu]kkiḻāṉettamāṉa koṭṭaikāṟaṉ vaṭakkaṭaya kuḻi

mōḻai kuḻi

maṉaiyum narasānāyakkarettu e-

ḻa viḷākattil cevvaripaḷḷam mataṉ

gōtrattu piḷḷaiyaṅkā ••••• nayiṉārayyaṅkāṟku rāja [s*]aundarya
vi[ḷā]kattu kāmpoti viḷākam kuḻi 23m cevvalli

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1456, Jayā, Mēsham, śu., 3, Friday, Mṛigasīrisha = A.D. 1534, April 17,
Friday.)

Gift of 300 kuḻis of land and two houses in the temple street to two
vaishṇava-brāhmaṇas for reciting the purāṇam, known as Bhakti Sañjīvini,
in the temple of Lakshmī Narasimhamūrthy in Kūvam, surnamed
Narasānāyakapuram in Chandragiri-rājyam, a subdivision of
Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, for the merit of Periyasvāmi Narasā Nāyakar.
Each one of the brāhmaṇas was given a monthly remuneration of 3
paṇams and 3 kalams of paddy. In addition to this they shall receive a
daily allowance of some specified quantity of sacred food (prasādam), betel
leaves and a monthly quota of sandal. Achyutadēvarāya proclaimed the
grant in the presence of Viṭṭalēśvara on the banks of the river
Tuṅgabhadra.

l.ta:
mum peṟṟa iṟṟai nāḷ
jeyaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu

vini sevikkiṟattukku kaṭṭaḷai
periraṇṭukku peṟ per oruttaṟku māsamoṉṟukku mūṉṟu paṇamum
mukkala nellu

mum aṭaikkāyamutu añcu
pākkum pattu veṟṟilaiyu māsamoṉṟukku ṉālu palac cāttuppaṭi
caṉtaṇamum

rai vārttuk
kuṭutta nilam kūvattu ēri kīḻe kuḻi muṉṉūṟukku viparam tirupuvaṉamā

ti teruvil teṉciṟakil
mutal maṉai raṇṭum āca

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of 320 kuḻis(?) of land to a brāhmaṇa,
Govindaiyyaṅgār, the son of Veṅkaḷaiyaṅgār, on the occasion of the
consecration of the image of Lakshmī Narasimhamūrti.

l.ta:
innilattu vivaram kūvattu Erikīḻe kīḻaicceriyil
rāceṉtiṟacoḻa viḷākattil vayirātarāyar kāṇiyil orucciṟakkaikku

••••• ttakaṉ kuṇṭil •••
maturāṉta viḻupparaiyar kāṇi kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: (Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Mēsham, ba., 3, Friday, = A.D. 1533, March 28, Friday.)

The inscription records the appointment of Chiṅgayaṉ, the son of
Mātinēthi Mallaiyar, as the watchman of the temple of Lakshmi
Narasimhamūrti in Narasānāyakkapuram by Tīttāra Piḷḷai of Kāñchipuram,
the secretary of the king. For his services in the temple, the watchman
was assigned one house, a monthly remuneration of 6 paṇams, a share in
the temple prasādam and some share in the dishes (paṇiyāram) from the
temple. He was also given a daily allowance of one uḻakku of oil for
lighting a lamp (vācal viḷakku) in front of the temple gate.

l.ta:
koyil
vācalkāvalukku mātineti mallaiyar makaṉ ciṅkayaṉukku kāṇiyāṭciyākak
kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭapaṭi māsam
tteruvil vaṭaciṟakil kīḻai maṉaikku aṭuttamelai maṉai oṉṟum

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1530, Sowmya, Mēsham, śu., 12, Pūram = A.D. 1609, April, 15,
Wednesday.).

Damaged and incomplete. Mentions the sīrmai of Gōburi Obarāja.
Seems to register a grant of 65 veṇkaṭarāyan varāgaṉ for the supply of
sacred food during the specified 14 festival days in a year in the temple
of Swāmi Aḻagiyasiṅgar at Kūvam also known as Tyāgasamudranallūr.

l.ta:
kālattile
jeyaṅkoṇṭacoḻa toṇṭa maṇṭalattu cantirakiri rā

ttu teṉkarai
maṇaviṟk koṭṭattil kaṇuṟūr ṉāṭṭilk kūvvamāṉa tiyākasamu

rājāviṉ cīrmai pūṇṭi timmavaramāṉa aṅkaḷam ma •••••

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the Nāchchiyar shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Records that an individual, Cīrāmadūtar, consecrated the images of
Perumāḷ, Pirāṭṭi and Iḷaiyāḻvān in the temple of Madurāntaka Viṇṇagar in
Madurāntakanallūr in Kanṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇayiṟ-kōṭṭam. The
Nagaram of Kaḍāraṅkoṇḍachōḻapuram in Pāsāli-nāḍu of Maṇayir-kōṭṭam
ageed to supply one uri of oil for lighting two lamps in the temple. (See
No. 245)

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaṉilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

vaṟku yāṇṭu muppattu nālāvatuk ketirāmāṇṭu
maṇayiṟ koṭṭattu pācāli nāṭṭuk kaṭāraṅkoṇṭa coḻapurattu nakarattom
ikkoṭṭattu-

ka viṇṇakaril nāṅkaḷ
cirāmatūtarai iṭṭu eḻuntaruḷivitta perumāḷukkum pirāṭṭiyāṟkum
iḷayāḻvānukkum tirunu

viḷakku iraṇṭukkum vaitta eṇṇai uri
ivveṇṇai uriyum eṅkaḷūṟ cekkile ca

rātittavarai
celuttukkaṭavomāka sammatittu vaittom kiṭāraṅ koṇṭacoḻapurattu
nakarattom ippaṭi kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭik koḷka ippaṭikku
kumārantai tāḻipuracūr eḻuttu || ippaṭikku cāttantai

vaṭukan oṟṟi eḻuttu ippaṭikku
māmpāk kiḻān eṭṭi cāttai eḻuttu ippaṭikku ceṟuvuḻān karuṇākaran
moṭi eḻuttu ippaṭikku veṭṭantai akattiūran araṅkan eḻuttu ippaṭikku
kaḷattūḻān •••ppan ponna eḻuttu ippaṭikku pāṭi kiḻān nīlakaṇṭi kovan
eḻuttu ippaṭikku kaṇṇa vāṇiyan āccan cāttai eḻuttu ippaṭikku • •

ippaṭikku ceṟuvuḻān karuṇākaran vaṭavāyil [eḻuttu] ippaṭikku
kaṇṇa

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the Nāchchiyār shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription is connected with the previous one (No. 244). Records
the consecration of the images of Perumāḷ, Pirāṭṭi and Iḷaiyāḻvār by
Paṇṇan alias Tāḻi Chūṟaiyān Cīrāmadūtan of Kiḍāraṅkoṇḍachōḻapuram in
Pāsāli-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇayiṟ-kōṭṭam and a gift for providing
sacred food and two lamps to the deities.

l.ta:
ikkoyilil perumāḷaiyum pirāṭṭiyāraiyum iḷaiyāḻvāraiyum
eḻuntaruḷuvittu ivarkaḷukku tiruvamutupaṭiyum tirununtā-

rātittavarai celvatākac ceyvitten
maṇayiṟ koṭṭattup pācāli nāṭṭu kiṭāraṅ-

koṇṭacoḻapurattil vāṇiyar paṇṇanāna tāḻicūṟaiyān cirāmatūtanen
itta

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the Nāchchiyār shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
The beginning of the inscription is lost. It is known from another
inscription of Kulōttuṅga I (No. 249), that the donor of this inscription,
namely Vāsudēvaṉ Rājasundaraṉ appears to be the father of Vayiramegan.
On this basis this inscription could be assigned to Kulōttuṅga I.

Records a sale of 4,000 kuḻis of land for a consideration of 30 aṉṟāḍu-
naṟ-kācu by the mahāsabha as iṟaiyili-dēvadāṉa to Vāsudēvaṉ Rājasundaraṉ,
a landlord of Iruṅgaṉūr. The inscription stipulates some regulations with
regard to irrigation facilities to the granted land. The taxes exempted are
antarāyam, veṭṭi-muṭṭaiyāḷ, cillirai and chōṟumāṭṭu. Mentions the land
measuring rod, patiṉaṟu-cāṇ-kōl.

l.ta:
kku eṅkaḷūrk kīḻeri etirnila mivaṉukku viṟpataṟku kīḻpāṟkellai
avve-
ri nīrkoppukku meṟku teṉpāṟ kellai ilampiyaṉkoṭṭūr
var tevatānattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟ kellai • • mpayāṉkoṭṭūr nila-

māṉa vitikkaḻanikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟ kellai ivverit
toṭṭuvāykkut-

teṟkum inṉāṟ perellaikku naṭuvupaṭṭa nilattil meṟkaṭaiyap
patinaṟucāṇ-
koṇṭu iṟaiyiḻicci yiṟaiyili tevatānamāka viṟṟuk kuṭuttom

iṉṉilattukkup paṇṭoṭi paḻanaṭai eṅkaḷūrēri nīrpāyvatākavuṅ kīḻeri nīr
nīrī

riṟaikkumiṭattu ivverikaṇ ūṭaṟuttu nīr pāyap peṟuvatākavum nīrvilai
a

yam veṭṭi muṭṭaiyāḷ cilliṟai coṟu māṭṭu uḷḷiṭṭu eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavuñ
colla

vuṅ kāṭṭavuṅ koḷḷavum peṟātomākavum ippaṭi cammatittu irukālāvatu
mukkālāva-
