inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the Nāchchiyār shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and contains a portion of the historical introduction of
Kulōttuṅga I.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaṇilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

maṇimuṭicūṭi Enai mannavar keṭṭu • toṭat
tikkaṉaittu

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the Nāchchiyār shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. Seems to register a sale of land by the assembly of
Kōṭṭur alias Chōḻavichchādira-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Maṇayiṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. Also mentions
Madurāntaka-nallūr, a village in the same kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
pūmālai miṭaintu ponmālai tikaḻtarat tārmālai malinta
parumaṇit tiraḷpuyat tirunilamaṭantaiyoṭu jayamakaḷiruppat taṉ vaḷamār

vantaṉateṉap peṟṟut tirumakaḷoru taniyiruppak kalaimakaḷ coṟṟiṟam
puṇarnta kaṟpiṉaḷāki viruppoṭu nāvakattiruppa akilapuvaṉaṅ kavippator
putumatipola veṇkuṭaimmicai ciṟappat ticaitoṟu

tikiriyoṭu
ceṅkol naṭappak karuṅkaliyoḷittu vaṉpilat tiṭaikkiṭappak
kuḷattiṭait teluṅka vīmaṉ vilaṅkal micaiyeṟa-

vuṅ kaliṅkar pūmiyaik kaṉaleri parukavum aimpaṭaip paruvattu vempaṭaitāṅki
veṅkai maṇṭalattiraṅki [yini]tiruntu vaṭaticaiyaṭippaṭuttaruḷit
teṉṟicait tarumamu

ḻaippa vetamum meymaiyumātiyukam
polat talaittalai ciṟappa va

ruḷi vilaṅkarum porpuliyāṉai pārttivar
cūṭa niṟaimaṇi makuṭam urimaiyiṟ cūṭi maṉṉuyirkkellā miṉṉuyirttāy
poṟṟaṇṇaḷi parappit taṉittaṉi pura

taṉ koyiṟ koṟṟavācaṟpuṟattu
maṇināvoṭuṅka muracukaṇ muḻaṅka vicaiyamum pukaḻum meṉ-

meloṅkac ceḻiyar veñcuram pukac ceralar kaṭal puka aḻitaru ciṅkaṇarañci
neñcalamarak kaliṅkar tiṟaiyiṭak kaṉṉaṭar veṉniṭak koṅkavarotuṅkak
koṅkaṇar cāya maṟṟetticai maṉṉaru

ttamakkaraṇenat tirumalarc
cevaṭiyurimai yiliṟaiñcat tollai ēḻulaku

ḻuteḻat toṉṟi
mullaivāṇakai mukkokkiḻānaṭi umaiyoṭu caṅkaraṉ imaiyaccimaiyat
tiru

uṭaniruppa aṅkavaṉ makuḻuṅ kaṅkaiyoppākit
terivaiyar tilata

vanitai tiripuvaṉamuṭaiyāḷ avantiruvuḷḷattaruḷ
muḻutuṭaiyāḷeṉavuṭaniruppa cempon vīrasi

rāna
cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vikkiramacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu ēḻāvatu
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu maṇaiyiṟ koṭṭattuk kaṉṟūrṉāṭṭuk
koṭṭūrāṉa coḻaviccātirac caturvetimaṅkalattu

yom
nilavilaiyāvaṇak kaiyyeḻuttu ik koṭṭattu maturā[n]takanallūr-

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the Nāchchiyār shrine in the Narasimha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of tax free land by the Ūrār of Madurāntaka-nallūr in Kaṉṟūr-
nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇayiṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, for
providing sacred food offerings to the deities Perumāḷ, Pirāṭṭi and Iḷaiyāḻvār
in the temple of Madurāntaka-Viṇṇagar Āḻvār in the village. The supply of
sacred food offering to the deity was entrusted to the servants of the
temple (dēva-kaṉmikaḷ).

l.ta:
nemi
naṭappa viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai yiḷaṅkopparuvattuc cakkarakoṭṭattu
vikkiramattoḻilāṟ putumaṇam puṇarntu matuvaraiyīṭṭam vayirākattu
vāriyayilmuṉaik kuntaḷavaraiyar ta

ṉamiriya vāḷuṟai kaḻittu toḷvali
kāṭṭip porppari naṭāttik kī[r*]ttiyai niṟutti vaṭaticai vākai cūṭit
te

r cūṭa munṉai manuvāṟu peruka
kaliyāṟu vaṟuppac ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ cella veṇkuṭai
yirunilaviḷākameṅkaṇu

ṉātu tirunila veṇṇilāt tikaḻa orutaṉi
meruviṟ puli viḷaiyāṭa vārkaṭaṟṟīpā

rattup pūvartiṟai viṭu tantakalañ
corikaḷiṟu muṟai niṟpa vi-

laittiṭat tan pon karipuṟattiṭaik kiṭappa
iṉnāṭpiṟ kulappiṟai pol niṟpiḻaiyennuñ colletir koṭā
veḻkulattaracar aḷattiyiṟpaṭṭa kaḷiṟṟintiṭṭamuṅ kallatar naṅkili tuṭaṅki
maṇṇūr naṭuvenat tuṅkapattiraiyaḷavu meṅkanum paṭṭa veṅkaḷiṟum
viṭṭatanmānamuṅ kūṟinavīramum naṭappa veṟinmalaikaḷu mutuku neḷippa iḻi

laivirittalamara kuṭa ticait
tan nāḷukātu tānu
ttāṉaiyum pannāḷiṭṭa palapala mutukum payattetir māṟiya jayapperu

paraṇi yiraṇṭum ōrvicai kaik koṇṭīṇṭiya pukaḻoṭum pāṇṭi maṇṭalaṅ
koḷḷa tiruvuḷḷattaṭaittu veḷḷa varu parittaraṅkamum poruparikka •••••

vaṭakaṭal tenkaṭalpaṭarvatu polat tan peruñ
ceṉai •••ppañcavaraivarup poruta po-

rkaḷattañci araṇenappukka kāṭaṟattuṭaittu nāṭaṭippaṭuttu maṟṟavar tammai
vaṉacarar tiriyum poṟṟaveñcuṟameṟik koṟṟa vijaya

mpanticaitoṟum
niṟutti muttincalāpamu muttamiṭpotiyinum matta veṅkari
paṭumaiyyaṟacaiyyaḻuṅka ••••• kaik koṇṭaruḷi tenṉāṭṭellai kāṭṭi
malai ñāṭṭuḷḷa cāveṟellā

ṉivicumpeṟa māveṟiya tanavarutanit
[ta]laivaraik kulakalar kulaiya •••••••••• neṟi toṟu nilaikaḷiṭṭaru-

yākamum viḷaṅkap
pār toḻac civaniṭattumeyenat tiyākavalli avanimuḻutuṭaiyāḷiruppa
yavaḷuṭan kaṅkai vīṟṟiru

ṟamaṅkayar tilatam ēḻicaivallapi
ēḻulakamuṭaiyāḷiruppa vāḻiyum avaṉi muḻutuṭaiyā[ḷo]ṭu
vīrasi

kaḷ śrī
kulottuṅkacoḻatevarkku yāṇṭu muppattu nālāvatu jayaṅko[ṇṭac]oḻa
maṇṭalattu

ka nallūr ūrom eṅkaḷūr śrī
maturā

ka viṇṇakarāḻvāṉ koyilil tiruvārātaṉai koṇṭaruḷukiṟa
perumāḷukkum pirāṭṭiyārkkum iḷaiyāḻvāṉukkum amutu ceytaruḷa
ucciyampo[tu] sa

onṟukkup perumāḷukkut tiruvamutaruci nānāḻiyum
pirāṭṭiyāṟkkut tiruvamutarici iru[nāḻiyum] iḷayāḻvānukkut tiruvamutarici
iru nāḻiyum āka arici kuṟuṇikku-

iraṇṭañcākki nāḷ oṉṟukku nellup patakku nā nāḻiyum kaṟiyamutu
iraṇṭukku nellu iru nāḻiyum neyyamutu āḻākkukku nel nānāḻiyum
tayiramutu nāḻikku nel nāḻiyum aṭaikkāyamutu veṟuṅkāy eṭṭukkum
veṟṟilai muppattiraṇṭu[kku] nel nāḻiyum āka nāḷ onṟukku nel
mukkuṟuṇi nānāḻikkum tiruvamutupaṭiyāka nāṅkaḷ iṟayili viṭṭa nilamāvatu
eṅkaḷūrt tiripuvanamātevip pe ••••• vaṭa kaṭayil viṭṭile-

ttattukku kīḻpāṟkellai ciṟṟāyar putūr ūrmeṭṭukku meṟkun
tenpāṟkellai veḷḷerikkum kīḻaippunnai ēttattukku vaṭakkum
melpāṟkellai pilāvettattukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
karuppacceṟuvukkum vanniceṟuvukkut teṟkum inān kellai ūrum
akappaṭṭa nilamum in[nu]m melai māvettattukku kīḻpāṟkellai kīḻai
māvettattukku meṟku tenpāṟkellai āvik kālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟ
kellai kīḻai vañci ēttattukku-

kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai pilāyettattukku teṟkum innānkellai
uḷḷuyakappaṭṭa nilamum āka ēttam iraṇṭukkum peciṉanta perellai
ūrumakappaṭṭa nilam viḻal veṭṭiyu

tevakanmikaḷē cantirātittavaraiyuṅ kaik koṇṭu mippaṭi amutu ceyvikka
kaṭavarkaḷākavum innilañcuṭṭi nāṅkaḷ eccoṟṟuk kūṟṟunellu veṭṭi
muṭṭaiyāḷuḷḷiṭṭu maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭana yātonṟum koḷḷavuṅ kāṭṭavum
peṟātomākavum innilañcuṭṭi aḻivu ceyvān keṅkekkuṅ-

kumarikkum naṭuvupaṭṭa pāvaka[na mākkaḷ] ceyta pāvattaip paṭuvanākavum
ipparicu tevakanmikaḷ itte •••••••••• rāki kaṭavaṉ ippaṭi amutu
ceyvikkak kaṭavarkaḷāka sammātaṅ koṇṭu ittevarkaḷukku innilam
iṟayiliyāka aṟavolai cetu kuṭuttom maturā

ka nallūr ūrārum ippaṭi
kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭik koḷak kaṭavarkaḷāka sammatittu la[k]caṇam
paṇṇik kuṭuttom maturā

ka nallūr ūrom ippaṭikkuk karaṇattān
[teṭiya]māneti eḻuttu |||—ippaṭikku maturā

n eḻuttu ippaṭikku kuṟumuṭicaṅkankāḻi eḻuttu ippaṭikku iruṅkaṇūr
kiḻavan rājacu

ran vayiramekan eḻuttu ippaṭikku iruṅkaṇūr kiḻavan
mātevan kaliyaru[call]āmuṭayān eḻuttu ippaṭikku kumārantaka cerananti
eḻuttu ippaṭi[kku] [pai]yana • yarimātevan eḻuttu ippaṭikku pāṭalaṅkiḻavan
tevanti[ra]n eḻuttu ippaṭikkuc ceya[va]m[pata]k kiḻān tāḻimalai
mātevaṭikaḷ eḻuttu ||-

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1910.)
NARASIṄGAPURAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in front of the main gōpura in the Narasimha-Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Remission of taxes from the village of Kūvam alias
Narasānāyakkarpuram, in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Teṉkarai Maṇaviṟ-
kōṭṭam in the Chandragiri-rājyam in favour of the Narasiṅgaperumāḷ
temple by Saṅkaranāyakkar [Li]ṅganāyakar for the merit of Chinna
Timmayadēva Mahārāja, son of Irāmarāsa Timmaya. The taxes exempted
are makamai, suṅgam and āḷ-amañji.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1910.)
IḌAIYARPĀKKAM (Eḍayarpākkam), SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK,
CHINGELPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
Built in at the bottom. Gift of two perpetual lamps to the temple of
Tiruppaḍakkāḍuḍaiyār in Purisai-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam by the king.

l.ta:
āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattup
pu-

ricai nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruppaṭakkāṭuṭaiyāṟkku perumāḷ
kulottuṅkacoḻate-

vatu itil iṭaiyaṉ cakkikkoṉ paṭukkaṉ
kaṭava viḷakku arai

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1910.)
IḌAIYARPĀKKAM (Eḍayarpākkam), SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK,
CHINGELPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 95 sheep by Vēlaṉ Madhurāntakaṉ from Kōṉēri in Kōṉēri-
nāḍu, a subdivision of Eyiṟ-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam to the
temple of Tiruppaḍakkāḍuḍaiya Mahādēva at Purisai in Purisai-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam. The sheep were received by the local
shepherd, Paṅkiyañchi. He agreed to provide one uḻakku of ghee in the
temple treasury for the maintenance of one lamp. Mentions that the
Chandrasēkharaṉ Ravi alias Chōḻēndrasiṅga Ācāriyaṉ constructed the stone
temple.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅkac cayamātu virumpa nilamakaṇilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīnavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara
Enaimannavaririyaluṟṟaḻitarat tikkanaittu

vaṟku
immaṇṭalattu eyiṟ koṭṭattuk koneri nāṭṭuk koneriyil aracūr
kiḻavan veḷān maturā

ivvāṭu toṇṇūṟṟaiñcum cāvāmūvāp perāṭāka
ittevar curapi iṭaiyan paṅkiyañciyen kaikkoṇṭu ittevar
paṇṭāratte nicatam uḻakku ney nānum en vaṟkattārum a-

ḷakka kaṭavomākavumitu muṭṭil muṭṭiraṭṭi neyyaḷakkak kaṭavenākac
cammatittāṭu toṇṇūṟṟaiñcuṅ kaikkoṇṭu ca

rātittar uḷḷaḷavum
aḷakkak kaṭavenānen paṅkiyañciyen itu cilālekai

ciṅka ācāriyanen ivai en eḻuttu
ittanmattaic celuttāte virota ninṟār
kaṅkaiyiṭai kumariyiṭaiceytār ceyta pāvattup paṭuvār itu

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1910.)
IḌAIYARPĀKKAM (Eḍayarpākkam), SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK,
CHINGELPUT DISTRICT.
On the south base of the Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
Built in at the bottom and incomplete. Seems to refer to an
assignment of cows as an alternative arrangement made by the Ūrar of
Purisai for maintaining (3/4) of a lamp in the temple of
Tiruppaḍakkāḍudaiyār. The land, measuring one vēli in extent, granted as
nundāviḷakkup-paṭṭi by Āṇḍamaichchāni, the daughter of Aruḷāḷa Bhaṭṭan,
from Iḍaiyarruppākkam alias Rājavichchādhara-chaturvēdimaṅgalam on an
earlier occasion became uncultivable due to water logging and therefore the
lamp could not be maintained, which necessitated the rearrangement.

l.ta:
vatu
puraṭṭāti māsattu iṭaiyāṟṟup pākkamāṉa irājavijātirac catuppe-

timaṅkalattu savaṉaṉ aruḷāḷa paṭṭaṉ makaḷ āṇṭamaiccāni āḷuṭaiyār
tiruppaṭakkāṭuṭaiyāṟku ittevar tevatāṉam puricaiyūril ūr pakkal

tirununtāviḷakku paṭṭiyākak koṇṭu viṭṭa nilam oruveliyum ēri
koḷātalālum kirāmāṉatamātalālum innilattukku kāṇiyāḷanāy
payiṟce-

yvārilāmal ivviḷakku viḻuntu kiṭantamaiyil innilattāl ivviḷakku
cellāmai ivvūrārai uṅkaḷ nilam nīṅkaḷē kaikkoṇṭu innilat-

tāluḷḷa tirununtāviḷakku mukkālukkum pacu viṭaveṇumeṉṟu colli
ivvūrār pacu viṭa ivvūrār pakkal iviḷakku mukkālukkum pacukkaikko-

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1910.)
IḌAIYARPĀKKAM (Eḍayarpākkam), SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK,
CHINGELPUT DISTRICT.
On the south base of the Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to be related to the previous
inscription (No. 253). Grant of 30 kācus by Āṇḍamaichchāni, the daughter
of Aruḷāḷa Bhaṭṭan for maintaining (3/4) of a lamp in the temple of
Tiruppaḍakkāḍuḍaiyār at Iḍaiyāṟṟuppākam alias Rājavichchādhirach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Purisai-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The shepherds declined to accept the money
and subsequently the dēvadāṉa village of Purisai sold 2,000 kuḻis of land
as iṟaiyili and probably agreed to maintain the lamp.

l.ta:
āvatu jeyaṅkoṇṭacoḻa
maṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattup puricaināṭṭu iṭaiyāṟṟup pākkamāna

vaṟkut tirunantāviḷakkukku nān
āṭuk[o]ḷḷavarakkāṭṭina kācu muppatukku mukkāl viḷakku celvitāka
āṭu koḷḷa kācu kuṭukka iṭaiyar ivvāṭu koṇṭu neyyaḷakka
māṭṭomenṟu iṭaiyarit tevar paṇṭārattu ik-

var tevatānam puricai ūrom ikkācu
tantu eṅkaḷḷūrrille pūmi koḷa nilavilaiyā[va*]ṇak kaiyeḻuttu

eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāna eḻuttip paṭṭiyil melaic ceṟuvum itanmelai tirukuḷam
itan vaṭakkil ••••••••••

nālu[m] ākat taṭi palavināl patināṟcāṇ kolāl kuḻi ira[ṇ]ṭāyiramum
itil uḷpaṭṭa kiṇa[ṟum] iṟaiyili tiru •••••••••• paṭṭamāka iṟaiyiḻicci
cilliṟai coṟumāṭṭu āḷamañci •••nīrvilai yantarāyam ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 255 of 1910.)
NANDIVANAM (Gūḍuvāñchēri), CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in the Gaṇēśa temple.
inscription.p:
Partly damaged. Sanskrit and in Grantha characters. Seems to record a
gift of two lamps in the public hall by one Chēkkiḻāṉ Kuṭṭēṟa, who had
the title Vīrakēsari.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1910.)
URATTŪR (Orattūr), CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the base of the Agastyēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1451 (wrong), Vikruti, Viruchchikam, śu., 5, Friday, Tiruvōṇam = A.D.
1530, November 25, Friday.).

The inscription records a sarvamānya gift of Paṉaittāṅgal, a hamlet of
Urattūr in Cheṅgāṭṭu-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam to
Tiruvagattīsuramuḍaiya Tambirāṉār, for the merit of Chellappar alias Vīra-
Narasiṅgarāya Nāyaka Sāḷuva-Daṇṇāyakkar. It was stipulated that the
income from the village shall be utilized by the Tambirānār for conducting
services.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1910.)
URATTŪR (Orattūr), CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the base of the Agastyēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription is in Telugu. Records the construction of maṇḍapas,
gōpuras, and installation of images in the Śiva and Vishṇu temples at
Orattūr, by a certain Avanigaddala Pattā[bhi]rāmuḍu. The impression of the
inscription could not be traceable.

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.

p.b: Kulōttuṅga III. Year 35 and 139th day: A.D. 1214.

inscription.p:
Registers the royal order of the king, granting 109 kaḻañcu of gold
to the temple of Mahāvishṇu at Tiruviḍavandai in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Kalyāṇapuraṅgoṇḍachōḻa-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam
for conducting monthly festivals on the occasion of Pūram nakshatram, the
birth austerism of Kulōttuṅga III. The inscription gives a long list of
officers including several levels of Adhikāris, Kaṇkānis, Varippottagam,
Mugaveṭṭi and other functionaries, who acted in the issuance of the royal
order.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. Mentions the mercantile body (Nagarattār) of Kōmaḷam
alias Vīrachōḻa-paṭṭinam in Paḍuvur-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūr-kōṭṭam.
The village Kōmaḷam could be identified with Kōvaḷam, a village situated
north of Tiruvaḍandai.

l.ta: ṭṭuk komaḷamāṉa vīracoḻa paṭṭiṉattu nakarattom tiruvi-

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
The Ūrar of Taiyyūr in Kumiḻi-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūr-kōṭṭam
received 15 kaḻañcus of gold from Nakkanādittan alias Ulōkamāṇikkach
Cheṭṭi, a merchant of Koḍungōḷūr in Malai-nāḍu and agreed to pay interest
in paddy. The sabhā of Tiruviḍavandai agreed to receive the paddy and to
provide food for 30 brāhmaṇas in the temple of Āḻvār at Tiruviḍavanḍai
of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, on the occasion of the first new moon day of every
month. The village Kōmaḷam could be identified with Kōvaḷam, a village
situated on the north of Tiruvaḍandai.

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Vallaṅkiḻāṉ Chāmichūṟṟi, a merchant of Mayilārppil, a taṉkūṟu in
Puliyūrk-kōṭṭam, installed the image of goddess in the temple of
Varāhadēva at Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu in Āmūrk-
kōṭṭam, and granted 15 kaḻañcus of gold for the supply of 90 nāḻis of
ghee as interest to maintain one perpetual lamp. The sabha and the ūr
assemblies of Tiruviḍavandai agreed to maintain the lamp. The village
Mayilārppil could be identified with Mylapore now a part of the Chennai
city.

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 43.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 87.

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Two residents of Talaicayanappuram alias Taiyyur installed an image
of Maṇavāḷapperumāḷ in the temple of Varākasvāmi at Tiruviḍavandai, a
dēvadāṉa in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam and granted 10
kaḻañjus of gold. The sabha and ūr assemblies agreed to supply annually
56 kāḍi of short variety of paddy (kuṭṭai nel) and maintain the grant.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 93 sheep for lighting one perpetual lamp in the temple of
Varākadēva at Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu and a
subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam, by Kambanmañjan alias Ukavārip-pēraiyar of
Talaicayaṉappuram alias Taiyyūr, a taṉkūṟu of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam. Viḷakkaṉ
Kaḍanāḍan, a shepherd from Tiruviḍavandai received the sheep and agreed
to provide one uḻakku of ghee daily for the maintenance of lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 196.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 30 kaḻañcus of gold by Athikai Uttamach Cheṭṭi, a merchant
from Tiruvoṟṟiyūr in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam, to the temple of Varākadēvar at
Tiruviḍavandai in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu. The ūr of Talaicayanappuram alias
Taiyyūr, in Kumiḻi-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam received the
gold and agreed to pay interest in oil and paddy annually to the
assembly of Tiruviḍavandai for lighting one lamp in the temple and for
feeding 35 brāhmaṇās for nine days on the occasion of the uttiram
festival celebrated in the month of Paṅguni. Mentions the grain measure
kachchippēḍu ninrāṉ marakkāl.

ṭikai iraṭṭa pāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalai nāṭuṅ kollamuṅ
kaliṅkamu eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamu

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 125.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 12 kaḻañcus of gold by Tiruvaḍigaḷ Vaikunthan from
Talaicayanappuram alias Taiyyūr, a taṉkūṟu in Āmūrk-kōṭṭam for a lamp.
The ūr and sabhā of Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam, received the gold and agreed to maintain the
lamp by providing an annual supply of 90 naḻis of ghee.

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of a piece of land at Paḍuvūr, by Naṅgai Vaḍugiyār, the wife
of Ālaththulāṉ Raṇakēsari for providing sacred food to the image of
Varākadēva, installed in the Āḻvār temple of Tiruviḍavandai in Paḍuvūr-
nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam, by the donor. The Nāṭṭār of
Paḍuvūr agreed to maintain the grant. The same donor has made
provision for another grant for lighting one perpetual lamp during the
evening service to Varākadēva in the same temple and the sabhā of
Tiruviḍavandai agreed to maintain the lamp.
Published in RI., No. 32.

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription is incomplete and contains an unfinished historical
introduction of Vīrarājēndra.

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
The ūr of Taiyūr alias Rājakēsarinallūr in Kumiḻi-nāḍu, a subdivision
of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam, which was a district of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam
received 10 kaḻañcus of gold from Vallaṅkilāṉ Chāmi Chūṟṟi and sold 6
mā of land for providing sacred food to the Śrī Varākadēvar in the
temple of Tiruviḍavandai. The ūr agreed to cultivate the land and pay 50
kalam of paddy to the sabhā of Tiruviḍavandai. The sabhā accepted to
supply sacred food during the midday service in the temple. Mentions
that one paṭṭi of land consisted of 1,000 kuḻis, measured by 16 (human)
feet rod (patinaṟu-cāṇ-kōl).

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
The ūr of Paḍuvūr in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam
received a gift of 16(1/8) kaḻañcus of gold from Dēvanārayanan alias
Tisaimāṇikkach Cheṭṭi, a merchant of Kadarpārpaḷḷi in Malai-nāḍu for
feeding brāhmaṇas in the temple of Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa of
Paḍuvūr-nāḍu. It also agreed to measure out 25 kalams of paddy to the
sabhā. The sabhā agreed to feed 12 brāhmaṇas daily for 30 days
commencing from Sankrānti during the month of Kumbha (February-
March). Mentions a grain measure equivalent to the grain measure of
Kōmaḷam (kōmaḷattu nārācakkāl), an important mercantile centre (Kōvaḷam)
situated close by.

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Twelve families belonging to the local community of fishermen
(paṭṭiṉavar) and all their descedants were ordained to do service to the
Vishṇu temple and to pay a tax amounting to 9 kaḻañcus of gold. The
sabhā and ūr agreed to utilize the money for celebrating a seven-day
festival called Rājarājadēvar-tirunāḷ on the occasion of Sataiyam (nakshatra)
in the month of Āvaṇi (the birth asterism of Rājarāja I), in the temple of
Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu in Āmūrk-kōṭṭam. The
inscription was engraved under the supervision of the controller of the
coast and an administrator of the Nāḍu.

tirumakaḷ polap perunilac celviyun taṉakkeyurimai pūṇṭamai
maṉakkoḷa kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaṟuḷi veṅkai nāṭuṅ kaṅka pāṭiyum

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west base of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 90 sheep by Mummuḍichōḻa Brahmādhirājar of Chirudāvūr in
Āmūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam, for a perpetual lamp to be
lighted in the Āḻvar temple of Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu
of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṅḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The local shepherd
received the sheep and agreed to measure out 90 nāḻis of ghee to the
temple and the local sabhā agreed to maintain the lamp.

l.ta:
maṉunītimuṟai vaḷara mānilattai potu nīkki ceṅkolāl karuṅkali
kaṭintu veṇkuṭai niḻal vīrasiṅṅāsanattu vīṟṟi-

p paṭuvūr nāṭṭu tevatāna tiruviṭavantai āḻvarkku ikkoṭṭattu āmūr
nāṭṭuc ciṟutavūrp pūtalic celvakkovala kūttanāṉa mummuṭicoḻa

viḷakku onṟukku viṭṭa cāvāmūvāp
perāṭu toṇṇūṟuṅ kaiykkoṇṭu i[t] tirunantāviḷakku onṟukku
nicatam uḻakku

neyyāka āṇṭuvarai toṇṇūṟṟu nāḻi ney āḻvār paṇṭārattu
aḷakka kaṭavenāka
ivvāṭu toṇṇūṟuṅ kaiykkoṇṭen ivvūr irukkum na

muṭṭāmai celuttuvarak kaṭavemānom ivvūr
cavaiyom itu śrī vayiṣṇavar

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: (Makara, śu., 2, Friday, Tiruvōṇam = A.D. 1307, January 6, Friday.).

Unfinished. Remission of certain taxes on the half vēli of land at
Neṉmali by the Nāṭṭār of Āyiravēlip-paṟṟu, in favour of the consort of
Varaha Nāyaṉār in the temple at Aśurakulakālanallūr alias Tiruviḍavandai
in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūṟ-kōṭṭam in the Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-
maṇḍalam. The remitted taxes were to be utilized for providing sacred
food to the deity during the chandhi service instituted by Māthāṇḍaṉ
Rācarācadēvaṉ a kiḻāṉ of Neṟkuṉṟam in Mēlūr-nāḍu, of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-
valanāḍu.

l.ta:
yum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa
maṇ-
vantai śrīvarāhanāyaṉār nācciyāṟku im maṇṭalattu ikkoṭṭattu
āyiravelip paṟṟil nāṭṭārom in nācciyāṟku immaṇṭalattup puliyū-
rk koṭṭattu kulottuṅkacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu melūr nāṭṭu neṟkuṉ-

ṟaṅ kiḻāṉ mātāṇṭāṉ irā[ca*]r[ā*]catevaṉ amutu ceyvikkiṟa

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
(Tulā, śu, 1, Sunday, Sōdhi (Svāthi) = A.D. 1258, September 29,
Sunday.)

Purchase of 3 vēlis of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi for a consideration of
200 paṇams by the temple trustees (Tānattār) in a public auction from a
woman, Piḷḷai Maṟaipēr Manikkam. The woman could not pay the fine
imposed by her supervisor (?) since she has no funds and therefore she
was willing to sell the kāṇi. There was nobody to purchase her kāṇi and
even the sabhā declined to buy it. Thereupon the Nāṭṭār and the ūrār
advised the Tāṉattār of the temple to purchase the land in public auction,
which the latter complied with. They sold an ornament for 200 paṇam
and with that money the kāṇi was purchased.

l.ta:
komāṟapaṉmar tirupuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vīrapāṇṭiyatevaṟkku
yāṇṭu

yum nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyum

ākak koḷḷuṅkoḷ eṉṟu tāṉattārkku nāṭṭā-

veliyum koṇṭom tāṉattārom paṇiyāl ivai

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 278 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of one aṉṟāḍu-naṟ-kācu by Paṅkanūṟ Chāṇi Chani, a brāhmaṇa
lady of Chirudavūr for sacred food offerings to be provided during the
ēkādasi day in the month of Mārkaḻi to the presiding deity in the temple
of Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa-brahmadēya of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu a subdivision
of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The money was received
by 8 members of the family of Iḷaṅkīraṉ from the locality and they
agreed to provide the sacred food as stipulated.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpan nilamakaḷṇilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
uri-

mayiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīnavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara vikkalan
ciṅṇaṉ melkaṭal pā-
kku amutu ceyvikkukaikku ivaḷ kuṭutta kācu onṟu anṟāṭu naṟkācu
iḷaṅkīraṉ kuṭiyom ci araṅkanum ikkuṭi cuppanamā

kaṉ mātaiyum kūḷaṉ celvanum paiyyanamanātittanum kaṭuvan cāttan
araṅkaṉ panṟiyum

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
In two fragments. The first fragment is much damaged and seems to
record a tax free grant of 3 vēlis of land to the goddess in the temple of
Tiruviḍavandai as tirunāmattukkāṇi. The second fragment mentions the
distribution of sacred food offering in the temple.
I Piece

pi[ra*]cātam aññāḻiyum ākap piracātam pākkum naṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 9 drammas for lighting a twilight lamp (sandhi-viḷakku) in
front of the presiding deity in the temple of Tiruvidavandai, a dēvadāna
of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu a subdivision of Āmūrk-kōṭṭam by Koṟṟiyammai, a
resident of Satyāsrayakulakāla-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam.

l.ta: ṭṭi koṟṟiyammai āmūr koṭṭattu paṭuvūr nāṭṭu tevatānam tiruviṭava • • •

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 142 kalams of paddy to the temple of [Tiruvi]davandai, by
Teṉnavan Pallavarāyaṉ, a kiḻāṉ of Tirumaṅgalam in Puḻal-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam for feeding 50 brāhmaṇas in the Kalichchiṅgaṉ-
maṭam. The local veḷḷāḷars and the bhāgavathars received the paddy and
agreed to measure out 71 kalams of paddy as interest into the temple
treasury to be utilized for feeding brāhmaṇas during the 12 Amāvasi days
in a year and for meeting other expenses.

urimaiyiṟ-
[ciṟanta] maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīnavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara ēṉ

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. Beginning and end portions damaged. Seems to record a
gift by a brāhmaṇa lady for some services in the temple on the ēkādasi
day during the month of Mārgaḻi. Mentions Chembūrk-kōṭṭam.

l.ta: •••••••••• naṟu ticaitoṟu vikaṟpa veṅkali nīkki mey ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Varāha Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 24 kalams of paddy by Arayan Ādināyakan alias
Tiruvaḻundūrnāṭṭu Mūvēndavēḷān, a native of Vāmanamaṅgai in
Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Rājanārāyaṇa-vaḷanāḍu of Chōḻa-
maṇḍalam, for feeding 12 itinerant (apūrvi) brāhmaṇas in the temple of
Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūr-kōṭṭam,
in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam during the first Amāvāsi in the month of
Kārttikai. It was agreed that the annual interest of 12 kalams of paddy
shall be utilized for feeding service in the temple.

puṇara urimaiyiṟ-

paṭiyeyūṭṭak kaṭavarkaḷāka kaikkoṇṭārkaḷ ittaṉmam śrī vayiṣṇava

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Makara, śu., 10, and Uttiraṭṭādi (irregular) = A.D. 1265.)

Unfinished. Seems to register the public sale of house sites in the
tirumaḍaiviḷāgam by the Tāṉattār of the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya
Nāyaṉār at Vittūr, the northern hamlet of Madhurāntaka-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a taṉiyūr in Jayaṅgoḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam to the kaikkōḷas
and dancing girls (dēvaraḍiyār).

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 285 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Kumbha, śu., 13, Friday, Uttirāḍam (irregular) = A.D. 1350.)

Built in at the end. Seems to record a sarvamānya gift of 2 vēlis of
land at Vittūr, the northern hamlet of Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a
village in Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam for undertaking
repairs and services in the temple. Mentions the land measure nāḍaḷavu-kōl.

l.ta:
yum ve[ḷ*]ḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirāṭattu
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu kaḷattūrk koṭṭattu śrī maturāntaka
satu

kaḷum pala upātikaḷum uḷppaṭa pūcai tiruppaṇikku nāṭaḷavukolāl
vittūrile iraṇṭu veli nilam

māṉiyamāka kuṭuttom iṉṉāḷ
mutal ippaṭi ceyka cantir[ā*]tittavarai •••••

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 286 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The king remitted taxes on lands and on cheṭṭis, kaikkōḷas, vāṇiyar,
cekku-vāṇiyar, and also on all the members designated as kācāyam group
(kācāya-vargam) and those settled in the quarters of the temple of
Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya Nāyaṉār at Vittūr in favour of the temple for
meeting the expenses on festivals and other services.

l.ta:
ttu putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa nāḷ vittūrāṉa
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūṟ koṭṭattu śrī maturāntakac
catu

tiruṉāmattukkāṇiyāṉa tirumaṭaiviḷākam nāṟpāṟkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa
tirumaṭaiviḷākam cūḻnta nañcai puñcaiyum ēṟum kuṭi ceṭṭikaḷ
kaikkoḷar kaṟṟai vaṭavāṇiyar cekkuvāṇikar ceṉaiyaṅkāṭi
kovilaṅkāṭi koliyar maṟṟum epperppaṭṭa kācāyavaṟkamu muṭpaṭa ippaṟṟil
koḷḷum pala varikaḷum

iṟaiyiliyāka uṭaiyār vittūruṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku
tirunāḷukkum tirupparivaṭṭattukku[m*] tiruviḷakkeṇṇaikku
tirumelpūccu tiruppaṇikkum cantirātitta-
varai celvatākak koṭuttom innāḷ mutal ippaṭi ceyvate §

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of three vēlis of land as kuḍinīṅgā dēvadāṉam, for conducting
daily worship and maintenance of the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya
Nāyaṉār at Vittūr to the Tāṉattār by the king. Since the dēvadāṉa granted
on an earlier occasion became defunct, the king made this new grant.
Mentions the land measure, nāṭṭa-aḷavu-kōl.

l.ta: pattil aiñcu viṭum aḷavukku teṭum nilam

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.

p.b: No king (Muttu Virappa Nayaka II). Vishu (Vrisha): A.D. 1641.

inscription.p:
Seems to refer to a grant of land as māḍāpathyam for the merit of
Vīrappa-nāyakkar Ayyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 289 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
The Dāsar and Tāṉattār, the managers of the Kētāyuthaṉār appointed
five women servants (cēvikkum peṇḍukaḷ) for doing temple service in the
temple of Vittūr Uḍaiyār. They were provided with houses, some lands
and certain privileges in the temple food for their services.

l.ta:
ketāyutāṉar
kāriyattukkukaṭavar tācarum tāṉattārum vittūr uṭaiyārk koyil
cevikkum peṇkaḷukku kalveṭṭu pūṟuvam cevikku peṇkaḷillāta
paṭiyā-

le varatarācar putucāka viṭuvitta akkanāyaṉ makaḷ appanācciku caṉṉati
teruvil teṉciṟakil kīḻai iraṇṭumaṉai viṭṭu mūṉ[ṟām*] maṉaiyum
ivaḷ taṅkai aṟamaḷatta nāccikku melaimaṉaiyum aḻakiyar makaḷ
[e]ṟamanaccikku

itil melai maṉaiyum kampar makaḷ aḻakiya nāccikku vaṭa ciṟakil
mūṉṟāmaṉaiyum veḷḷantāṅkiṉā makaḷ poṉṉāku kīḻai maṉaiyum ki • • •

kīḻpātiri [pu] nūṟ[ṟu] irupatu kuḻiyum niṉṟap peṟ perile nūṟu kuḻiyum
koyile nāḻi coṟum cantirā

ttavaraiyum immatikā peṟṟu koyil cevukum kā •••cevittu [naṭattak]
kaṭavarkaḷākavum [ita]ṉ •••cukattile i[ru]k •••••

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 290 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p: (Karkaṭaka, śu., 5, Monday, Tiruvōṇam (irregular) = A.D. 1265.)

Gift of 6 cows for two lamps to be lighted in the temple of
Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya Nāyaṉar at Vittūr, the northern piḍagai of
Madhurānthaka-caturvēdimaṅgalam by Pushpagiri Jīyar. The śiva-brāhmaṇas
of the temple who held the right to perform duties in forty days cycle
received the cows and agreed to maintain lamps.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 291 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records that the maṇḍapa was built by Timmarācaiyyaṉ of
Nāraṇapura for the merit of Malaiyappaiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 292 of 1910.)
TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruvālīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
For the merit of Timmarasaiyaṉ, son of Rāmarāchaiyaṉ of
Nāraṇapura, the ūr and Tāṉattār of Kishanappar gifted one house and 120
kuḻis of land to Pichchiṉāya Ammai, the daughter of Piḷḷaiyar Naṭṭuvan,
a lady who took up service under the Tambirāṉār in the temple of
Tiruvālakōyiluḍaiyār.

l.ta:
nāraṇapuram irāmaracaiyaṉ
puttiraṉ timmaracaiyaṉ taṉmam āka tiruvālakkoyil uṭaiya tampirāṉāṟku

piḷḷaiyar naṭṭuvaṉ makaḷ picciṉāya ammaic cevukam āka kiṣṇappar ūr
tāṉattār ivaḷukku can(ṉa)ṉati teruvile kampaṉ mutal maṉaiyum nilam
mutal

ttavaraiyum
naṭakattākavum itukku ūrāṉ tāṉattār irācatar iyātoruvar akutam
coṉṉāl

taṉ tāyai tāṉē peṇṭu vaiyittirunta toṣattile pokka kaṭavar āka
§

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 293 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records the rates of assessment of taxes like paṭṭadai-nūlāyam,
pērāyac-cammādam, daṇṇāyakkar-makamai and other taxes levied on cheṭṭis,
kaikkōḷas, vāṇiyars settled in the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple of
Tiruppulippagava Nāyaṉār.
Published in KANMK., Nos. 37/2005, p. 129.

l.ta:
ku tiruppulippakava nāyiṉār tirumaṭaiviḷākam pulippakavar
koyil

ceṭṭiyaḷ kaikkoḷar vāṇiyar perkkum paṭṭaṭai nūlāyam
āṭṭaicammātam
perāyac cammātam kaiyeṟpu atikaimāta mātaviraṭṭi taṇṇāyakkar makamai

maṟṟum āyattai nokkik koḷḷum pala upātiyum uṭpaṭa āṇṭu
oṉṟukku per

itukku akitam coṉṉār uṇṭu ākil keṅkaik karaiyil kārām
pacuvaik koṉṟāṉ

pāvamuṅ koṇṭu tāṉ paṇiṉa [cu]k[tai]mum taṉakku utavātāmākak
kaṭavatu ākavum ippaṭikku ivai

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 294 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records that, a reduction of the levy called vāchalpaṇam was granted
by the king, on a request preferred by the residents of tirumaḍaiviḷāgam
of the temple of Pulipparkōil, while the king was on a visit to the
Chandragiri-cāvaḍi. Accordingly, the Taṉattār of the temple reduced the
rates of vāchalpaṇam on looms (taṟi), betel leaf sellers (cēṉaikkadai), oil
mongers (kachchavaḍa-vāṇiyar), fishermen (śivaṉpaḍavar) and other
communities settled in the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple.
Published in KANMK., Nos. 36/2005, p. 128.

l.ta:
līcuraṉ śrī vīra vijayapūpatirāya uṭaiyaṟkkuc
cellāniṉṟa Eviḷampi varuṣa

kava nāyiṉar tirumaṭaiviḷākam pulippakavar koyil tāṉattār ivvūr
kaikkoḷarkku kalveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭi nāṭṭil koyippaṟṟa aṟṟamari-

āti vācalppaṇam kaḻikkaveṇum eṉṟu puṟanāṭṭile cantirakiric
cāvaṭiile poyirukkaiil pū
ku vācalpaṇa mūṉṟum kaḻittu kaṇṭataṟikku taṟi oṉṟukkuk koḷḷum

m iṟukka
kaṭavārkaḷ ākavum upāti viniyokam nāṭṭil koyilppaṟṟu
aṟṟa mariāti iṟukkak kaṭavarkaḷ ākavum immariāti ca

varaiyum
naṭakkum paṭikkuk kal veṭṭikkuṭuttom ivvūr ūravaṟku

uṭaiyā[r*]t tiruppulippakava nāyiṉār koyil tāṉattārom śrī
māhe

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 295 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1374, Āṅgīrasa, Vrichchika (Kārttikai), śu., 5, Tiruvōṇam = A.D. 1452,
November 16, Thursday.).

Records that Kaṇakkaṉpaṭṭu alias Chēdirāyananallūr, a tirunāmattukkāṇi
village was granted as dēvadāṉa-iṟaiyili to the temple of Tiruppulippagava
Nāyaṉār at Pattūr, the northern hamlet of Madhurāntaka-
chaturvēdimaṅglam in Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam, a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-
maṇḍalam, by Dēvappagaḷ, son of Mahāpradāni Annadāta Daṇṇāyakkar for
the health of the king. The income from the taxes like poṉ-vari, dāṉiya-
vargam, and other levies from the village shall be utilized for the early
morning wakeup service (tiruppaḷḷi-eḻuchchi), for smearing of ungunts on
the body of the god and other services in the temple.
Published in KANMK., Nos. 27, 29/2005, pp. 119, 121.

l.ta:
ṉ mel cellā
niṉṟa āṅkira varuṣam

pakṣattu pañcamiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu kaḷattūṟ-

timaṅkalattu vaṭapiṭākai pāttūr
uṭaiyār tiruppulippakava nāyināṟku śrīmaṉ-

vappakaḷ irāyar
tirumeṉikku naṉṟāka kaḷattūr paṟṟu kaṇakkaṉ paṭṭu āṉa

cētirāyanallūr tiruṉāmattukkāṇi āṉa intak kiṟāmam tevatāṉa
iṟaiiliyākak kuṭuttom inta ūril uṇṭu āṉa poṉvaṟka

tāṉiyavaṟkam maṟṟum antap paṟṟil naṭakkum paḻavari putuvarikaḷum cakala
camutāyap piṟāttiyum iṉṉāyiṉāṟku tiruppaḷḷieḻuccik kuṟaivaṟuppukkum

aṅkaraṅka pokattukkum pūcai tiruppaṇikkum iṉṉāḷ mutalāka
cantirātittavaraikkum naṭakkumpaṭikku

ākak kuṭuttom intat taṉmattukku akitam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaik
karaiyil kārām pacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile pokak-

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 296 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Grant of certain privileges in the temple to 20 families and
their descendants, who were assigned to the temple of Tiruppulippagavar by
the tāṉattār, māhēshvaras, kaikkōḷas and kaikkōḷa mudalis in the presence
of Kaṇḍiyadēvar, probably an official representative of the king.

l.ta:
tiruvāykkeḻvi muṉṉāka komāṟapaṉmar tiripuvaṉac
cakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu

vatu uṭaiyār
tiruppulippakavar koyilil śrī māhecuramum tāṉattāru-

m kai[k*]koḷarum tevar kaṇṭiyatevar muṉpāka keṭṭu peṇṭukaḷ
makkaḷum puttira puttirarum eṅkaḷil mutalmai aṭaivu niṉṟapaṭi amarakoṉum
vēṇāvuṭaiyāṉum pallavaṉ mārāyaṉum atiyamā-

ṉum nantipaṉmaṉum iyātavarāyaṉum maṉamottakaṇṭa mārāyaṉum
muṉaiyataraiyaṉum tirukkāḷatti uṭaiyāṉum ālālacuntara mārāyaṉum
cempiyataraiyaṉum pañca[va]rrāyaṉum

teṉṉavataraiyaṉum mīṉavataraiyaṉum mālaiiṭṭāṉ kāmatevaṉ
coḻakoṉ vattarāyaṉ mu ••••• koṉ •••••••••• kkunāyaṉum ākapper
irupatu ••••• ik koyilil cuta-

ntiramum ūril cutantiramum ivarkaḷ tiruvolakkattil veṟṟilai peṟavum
nilam taṅkāta puttira puttira ••••• tevaraṭiyāṟku oṭukkavum peṇṭukaḷ
makkaḷ ••••• tānattār uṇṭupokavum ippaṭi ca-

ntirātittavarai cellakkaṭavu[tā*]ka cammatittom ivūr tāṉattārum
cimāyecurarum kaikkoḷarum kaikkoḷa mutalikaḷum ivaṉaivvarom
tānattārku mā[yecurar]kum camainta nāṟpatteṇṇāyira veḷāṉ eḻuttu

ūrāṟ[k*]ku camainta apimāṉapūcaṇa veḷān eḻuttu kāṭavataraiyar
vaḷavakoṉār iruṅkoḷar ceramāṉār kaṇṭiyatevar

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 297 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1327, Pārthiva, Mēsham, śu., 4, Friday, Rōhiṇi = A.D. 1405, April 3,
Friday.)

Damaged. Seems to assign the status of a dwelling quarter (graha-
kshētram) on the village of Agaram Vaḻudippākkam, the northern hamlet
of Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Jayankoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu śrī maturāntaka catu

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 298 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vyāghrapadēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Rishabha, śu., 12, Monday and Sōdhi (Svāthi) = A.D. 1344, May 24,
Monday.)
Published in KANMK., No. 28/2005, p. 120.

A sarvamāṉya gift of taxes, including taṟik-kaḍamai and pēr-kaḍamai
payable by the inhabitants including kaikkōḷar, chāliyar, vāṇigar,
chēṉaiyaṅgāḍi, kōyilaṅgāḍi, who settle within the boundaries of the
tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple of Tiruppulipagava Nāyaṉār at Pattūr, the
northern hamlet of Madhurānthakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaḷattūrk-
kōṭṭam of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, to the temple for conducting
worship, festivals and other services.

l.ta:
yum tiṅkaṭ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa coti
nāḷ jayaṅ-

koṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūrk koṭṭattu śrī maturāntakac
catu

vaṭapiṭākai pāttūr uṭaiyār tiruppulippakava nāyanāṟku tirumaṭaiviḷākam
pulippakavar koyil

nāṉkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa kaikkoḷar cāliyar vāṇikar ceṉaiyaṅkāṭi
koyilaṅkāṭi maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa

puṟakkalaṉaiyum uṭpaṭa muṉpu uḷḷa kuṭiyum piṉpu ēṟum kuṭiyum
ēṟṟikkoṇṭu taṟikkaṭamai peṟkaṭamai maṟ-

ṟum ippaṟṟil koḷḷum palavarikaḷum pala upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa
iṉṉāyaṉāṟku pūcaikkum tiruppaṇikkum tirunāṭciṟappukkum iṉṉāḷ
mutal cantirātittavaraiyum

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
(Śaka 1316 (wrong), Īshvara, śu., ēkādasi, Thursday and Rēvathi = A.D. 1397,
March 29, Thursday.).

Built in at the bottom and incomplete. Seems to refer to a grant (?)
of Nāraṇavēḷāṉ to the temple of Tiruppulippagava Nāyaṉār in Pattūr, a
hamlet of Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam of
Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.
Published in KANMK., Nos. 39/2005, p. 133.

l.ta:
ṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuvairāyar
kaṇṭaṉ mūvairāyar kaṇṭaṉ

vati nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇ[ṭalattu] kaḷattūrk
koṭṭattu

[ṭaiyār] tiruppulippakava nāyiṉārkku veṇkuṉṟa ••••• kāḻiyūr nā(nā)ṭṭu
kūttap paṟ[ṟu taḷiyuḷ] tiruvāli[ru]kai • yi nā

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 300 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Under the orders of Maṅgappa Uḍaiyār, Pemmarasar assigned two
looms on the western street of the temple of Pulippa[ga]var and all the
income accruing from it to Iḷaiyaṉāyaṉār.

l.ta: uttaram vaṭapiṭākaip paṟṟup pulippavar koyil melaite-
ruvil iḷaiyaṉāyaṉārkku viṭṭa taṟiyiraṇṭukku varum ā-
yamum āyattai nokki koḷḷumupātayum yiy nāyiṉār-

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 301 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in the Vyāghrapādēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged at the end. On the upper portion of the slab
is a carving of a trisula in the centre, a bull and cock (?) are on its right
side and a ploughshare, an unidentified tool (?) and a lamp on the left.
Seems to refer to a gift of some pieces of land in the village of Āvimāṉa
Vātāpinallūr, a dēvadāṉa of the temple of Tiruppulippagavar at Pattūr, to
the temple of Tiruveṅkaḍuḍaiya Nāyaṉār in Madhurāntaka-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam. Mentions irrigation canals, Cīdēvi-vāykkāl, and
Āṟṟukkāl.
Published in KANMK., Nos. 38/2005, pp. 130-132.

edition.l: m[ci]paṇṭārattil [ poṉ-
ṉi] ṭṭuk koṇṭu tevatā-

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in a street in the village.
inscription.p:
Gift of taxes kaḍamai, ūr-viṉivōgam and ayam levied on the settlers
of the southern street in Pulipparkō[yi]l for the health of Narasiṅgarāsa
Uḍaiyār, by Perumāḷ, the manager of Timmarāsa Uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 302A.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1910.)
PULIPPARKŌYIL, MADHURANTAKAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in a street in the village.

p.b: No king or date. 17th cent. A.D. (?)

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record the grant of some provisions for
conducting worship in the temple of Pulipparkōvil.

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1910.)
NAGAR, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nāgaveṉṉa-Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Under the orders of the king, conveyed through a tirumugam, the
local chief (Nagara-kiḻāṉ) Ulagaḷandachōḻa Mūvēndavēḷāṉ granted lands and
taxes as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam for sacred food and other services in the temple of
Nāḍuḍaiya Perumāḷ in Nagar alias Dhīnachintāmaṇinallūr in Oymā-nāḍu
alias Vijayarājēndra-vaḷanāḍu. It is stated that a total of 600 kuḻis of land
in the village which was assigned on an earlier occasion to a dēvadāṉa
were excluded from the present grant. The taxes exempted in favour of
the temple include kaḍamai, kuḍimai, kācāyam, taṟiyiṟai, taṭṭār-pāṭṭam,
cekkuk-kaḍamai and ērimīṉ-pāṭṭam. The official order was attested by two
officers, Kappalūr Uḍaiyāṉ and Vāṇādarāyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1910.)
NAGAR, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north, west and south bases of the Kailāsanātha temple.
inscription.p: (Śaka 1379, Dhātri, Mīṉa, śu., 10, Monday (irregular) = A.D. 1456.).

Damaged and incomplete. Seems to refer to a grant made by
Narasiṅgayadēva, a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara to the temple of Kayilāyamuḍaiya
Nāyaṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 305 of 1910.)
NAGAR, TIRUKKOYILPUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On a slab set up in a street in the village.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Appears to be a grant of taxes kāval-suvandiram, chōṟumāṭṭu
and amañci, from Nagari by Muḷabākkamu Nāyakar to the temple of
Muḷubākkamuḍaiya Mahādēvar.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 306 of 1910.)
NAGAR, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On another slab set up in a street in the village.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 80.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 307 of 1910.)
ĀMŪR, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On a boulder in a field in the village.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Chēdiyarāyar of the Nāḍu, administrators of
the village jointly granted 3(3/4) vēlis of land to the temple of
Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiya Mahādēva at Āṉmūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi Mēlāṉmūr-
nāḍu for providing sacred food offering. The tax pāḍi-kāval was also
exempted in favour of the temple. The inscription also refers to an earlier
grant of land made by Kulōttuṅgachōḻa Kāḍavarāyar for providing a lamp
in the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1910.)
NĀLŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the door-posts at the entrance into the Plāśapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 309.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1910.)
NĀLŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the door-posts at the entrance into the Plāśapurīśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Mutilaled and the right side of the inscription is completely lost.
Seems to record a gift of 90 sheep by Māḍilaṉ Duvēdi-cheṭṭi •••Sōmāsi, for
lighting a lamp in the temple of Samaparēśvarattu Mahādēva at [Nalur], a
brahmadēya in [Chē]ṟṟūr-kūṟṟam. The 90 sheep were distributed to three
families of shepherds.

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 310 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Built in on both sides. The purport of the inscription is not
clear. Mentions Chēṟṟur-kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 311 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Fragment and built in at the top. Appears to be an iṟaiyili grant of
land by the sabhā to Brahmīśvarattu Mahādēva for maintaining two
perpetual lamps in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 312 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 370.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 313 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record an undertaking by the śiva-brāhmaṇas of
the temple of Tirumayāṉamuḍaiya Paramasvāmi at Nālūr in Chēṟṟūrk-
kūṟṟam, a subdivision of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu to maintain eight lamps
daily on receipt of money from an individual Dēvabhaṭṭaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 314 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Unfinished and built in at the top and left side. Seems to record a
grant of land for providing sacred food to the deity in the temple of
Agastīśvaram Uḍaiyār by the mahāsabha of Nālūr, a brahmadēya in
Chēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 315 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Fragment and built in at the top and damaged. Seems to record a
sale of wet land and the sabhā of Nālūr remitted the taxes by receiving
12 kācus from three individuals from the locality.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 371.

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara
temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems that a group of [śiva-brāhmaṇas] of the temple of
Tirumayāṉa[m Uḍaiyār] in Nālūr alias Vāṉavanmādēvich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Chēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam, a subdivision of [Kulōttuṅga] chōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu accepted money and agreed to maintain one perpetual lamp in
the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 452.

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 319 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 165.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. 320 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 160.

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 321 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 90.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to record a gift of land for sacred food offerings
to Lakshmi Rāghavadēvar by Duvēdi Būdanirāmadēvan and Duvēdi
Mādēvaṉ Cheṭṭi, of Nālūr, a brahmadēya in Chēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam in
Kshatriyasikhāmaṇi-nāḍu. Mentions that the village assembly met in the big
hall, Śrī Rājarājan, situated in front of the Samaparīśvarattu Mahādēva
temple. Irrigation channels, Śrīrājarāja-vāykkal, Samparīśvara-vāykkal and a
tank, Kollarkaḷ-kulam are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a sale of land by the
assembly of Vāṉavaṉmādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Chēṟṟūrk-kūṟṟam, a
subdivision of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa-vaḷanāḍu for instituting a temple garden.
Mentions a land transaction made during the 41st year of the king and the
temple of Samparīśvaramuḍaiyar. Lands belonging to the village goddess
(ūr piḍāri) and potter (vēṭkōvaṉ) and an irrigation channel, Olōgamādēva-
vāyakkāl are mentioned.

vatu miruccika nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu tacemiyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum
peṟṟa •••tti nāḷ kulottuṅkacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu ceṟṟūrk kūṟṟattu
vāṉavaṉ mātevic caruppeti maṅkalattu irukkum veṭkovaṉ

var
nilattukku vaṭakku ūrppiṭāriyār nilattukkun tirunantavanattukkum kiḻakkum

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. 324 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a tax free gift of land to the temple of
Tirumayāṉattu-paramasvāmi by the members of a brahmadēyam in
Ch[ēṟṟ]ur-kūṟṟam. Mentions the temple of Samaparēśvarattu-perumāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land for a perpetual lamp. The
mahāsabha, on receipt of 4 kācus from a veḷḷāḷa, Irāmaṉ Kūttaṉ remitted
taxes on the land.

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Beginning lost and portions damaged. Seems to refer to an iṟaiyili
grant of land to the shrine of Veṇṇaikkūttāḍi-aruḷugiṉṟa Āḷvār (Krishṇa) in
Tirunārāyaṇa Viṇṇagar of Nālūr, a brahmadēya in Chēṟṟūrk-kūṟṟam, a
subdivision of Kshatri[ya]śikhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu. Mentions that the assembly
met at the big hall called Gaṇḍarādittaṉ.

aruḷukiṉṟa āḷvārkkut ti ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incompete. Appears to refer to a gift of land to the
temple of Sama[parē]śvarattu-perumānaḍigaḷ at Nālūr, by the assembly of
Akkiramakōṭṭa-[chaturvēdi]maṅgalam a brahmadēya in Teṉkarai
Tirunaraiyūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 393.

No. 329A.

No. 330.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 254.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of two lamps by Tiruveṇkāṭṭu Naṅgai from Chembiyaṉ-kuḍavāyil
alias Ālampālil to the temple of Tirumayāṉam Uḍaiyar at Nālūr, in
Chēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam, a subdivision of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The śiva-
brāhmaṇas of the temple undertook to maintain lamps.

l.ta: m ik koyiṟ kāṇi uṭaiya civap-

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYÑĀNAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Jñānaparamēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Registers the resolution of the assembly of Nālūr alias
Vānavaṇmādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam not to engage certain persons for doing
the temple services. It further declared that those persons who act against
the resolution are enemies of the village (grāmadrōhin).

l.ta: ai paṇṇinapaṭi nammūril ūrār na[m]mūrkkum nam
ivarkaḷ aruḷi ceyya eḻutiṉen ūṟ kaṇakku
